error) {\n\t\tpod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, nodeName := range nodeNames {\n\t\t\tif pod.Spec.NodeName == nodeName {\n\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// PodUnschedulable returns a condition function that returns true if the given pod\n// gets unschedulable status of reason 'Unschedulable'.\nfunc PodUnschedulable(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {\n\treturn func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n\t\tpod, err := := podutil.GetPodCondition(&pod.Status, v1.PodScheduled)\n\t\treturn cond != nil && cond.Status == v1.ConditionFalse &&\n\t\t\tcond.Reason == v1.PodReasonUnschedulable && pod.Spec.NodeName == \"\", nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// PodSchedulingError returns a condition function that returns true if the given pod\n// gets unschedulable status for reasons other than \"Unschedulable\". retry.\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\t_, cond := podutil.GetPodCondition(&pod.Status, v1.PodScheduled)\n\t\treturn cond != nil && cond.Status == v1.ConditionFalse &&\n\t\t\tcond.Reason != nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// WaitForPodUnschedulableWithTimeout waits for a pod to fail scheduling and returns\n// an error if it does not become unschedulable within the given timeout.\nfunc WaitForPodUnschedulableWithTimeout(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod, timeout time.Duration) error {\n\treturn wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, timeout, 30*time.Second)\n}\n\n// WaitForPodSchedulingGated waits for a pod to be in scheduling gated state\n// and returns an error if it does not fall into this state within the given timeout.\nfunc v1.Namespace{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: n, Labels: labels}}\n\t\tif _, err := cs.CoreV1().Namespaces().Create(context.TODO(), &ns, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn     82→// ShutdownFunc represents the function handle to be called, typically in a defer handler, to shutdown a running module
    83→type ShutdownFunc func()
    84→
    85→// StartScheduler configures and starts a scheduler given a handle to the clientSet interface
    86→// and event broadcaster. It returns the running scheduler and podInformer. Background goroutines
    87→// will keep running until the context is canceled.
    88→func StartScheduler(tCtx ktesting.TContext, cfg *kubeschedulerconfig.KubeSchedulerConfiguration, outOfTreePluginRegistry frameworkruntime.Registry) (*scheduler.Scheduler, informers.SharedInformerFactory) {
    89→	clientSet := tCtx.Client()
    90→	informerFactory := scheduler.NewInformerFactory(clientSet, 0)
    91→	evtBroadcaster := events.NewBroadcaster(&events.EventSinkImpl{
    92→		Interface: clientSet.EventsV1()})
    93→	go func() {
    94→		<-tCtx.Done()
    95→		evtBroadcaster.Shutdown()
    96→	}()
    97→
    98→	evtBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(tCtx.Done())
    99→
   100→	logger := tCtx.Logger()
   101→
   102→	sched, err := scheduler.New(
   103→		tCtx,
   104→		clientSet,
   105→		informerFactory,
   106→		nil,
   107→		profile.NewRecorderFactory(evtBroadcaster),
   108→		scheduler.WithKubeConfig(tCtx.RESTConfig()),
   109→		scheduler.WithProfiles(cfg.Profiles...),
   110→		scheduler.WithPercentageOfNodesToScore(cfg.PercentageOfNodesToScore),
   111→		scheduler.WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds(cfg.PodMaxBackoffSeconds),
   112→		scheduler.WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds(cfg.PodInitialBackoffSeconds),
   113→		scheduler.WithExtenders(cfg.Extenders...),
   114→		scheduler.WithParallelism(cfg.Parallelism),
   115→		scheduler.WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(outOfTreePluginRegistry),
   116→	)
   117→	tCtx.ExpectNoError(err, "creating scheduler")
   118→
   119→	informerFactory.Start(tCtx.Done())
   120→	informerFactory.WaitForCacheSync(tCtx.Done())
   121→	err = sched.WaitForHandlersSync(tCtx)
   122→	tCtx.ExpectNoError(err, "waiting for handlers to sync")
   123→	logger.V(3).Info("Handlers synced")
   124→	go sched.Run(tCtx)
   125→
   126→	return sched, informerFactory
   127→}

   129→func CreateResourceClaimController(ctx context.Context, tb ktesting.TB, clientSet clientset.Interface, informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory) func() {
   130→	podInformer := informerFactory.Core().V1().Pods()
   131→	claimInformer := informerFactory.Resource().V1().ResourceClaims()
   132→	claimTemplateInformer := informerFactory.Resource().V1().ResourceClaimTemplates()
   133→	features := resourceclaim.Features{
   134→		AdminAccess:     true,
   135→		PrioritizedList: true,
   136→	}
   137→	claimController, err := resourceclaim.NewController(klog.FromContext(ctx), features, clientSet, podInformer, claimInformer, claimTemplateInformer)
   138→	if err != nil {
   139→		tb.Fatalf("Error creating claim controller: %v", err)
   140→	}
   141→	return func() {
   142→		go claimController.Run(ctx, 5 /* workers */)
   143→	}
   144→}

   146→// StartFakePVController is a simplified pv controller logic that sets PVC VolumeName and annotation for each PV binding.
   147→// TODO(mborsz): Use a real PV controller here.
   148→func StartFakePVController(ctx context.Context, clientSet clientset.Interface, informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory) {
   149→	pvInformer := informerFactory.Core().V1().PersistentVolumes()
   150→
   151→	syncPV := func(obj *v1.PersistentVolume) {
   152→		if obj.Spec.ClaimRef != nil {
   153→			claimRef := obj.Spec.ClaimRef
   154→			pvc, err := clientSet.CoreV1().PersistentVolumeClaims(claimRef.Namespace).Get(ctx, claimRef.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   155→			if err != nil {
   156→				// Note that the error can be anything, because components like
   157→				// apiserver are also shutting down at the same time, but this
   158→				// check is conservative and only ignores the "context canceled"
   159→				// error while shutting down.
   160→				if ctx.Err() == nil || !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   161→					klog.Errorf("error while getting %v/%v: %v", claimRef.Namespace, claimRef.Name, err)
   162→				}
   163→				return
   164→			}
   165→
   166→			if pvc.Spec.VolumeName == "" {
   167→				pvc.Spec.VolumeName = obj.Name
   168→				metav1.SetMetaDataAnnotation(&pvc.ObjectMeta, pvutil.AnnBindCompleted, "yes")
   169→				_, err := clientSet.CoreV1().PersistentVolumeClaims(claimRef.Namespace).Update(ctx, pvc, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
   170→				if err != nil {
   171→					if ctx.Err() == nil || !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   172→						// Shutting down, no need to record this.
   173→						klog.Errorf("error while updating %v/%v: %v", claimRef.Namespace, claimRef.Name, err)
   174→					}
   175→					return
   176→				}
   177→			}
   178→		}
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	pvInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
   182→		AddFunc: func(obj interface{}) {
   183→			syncPV(obj.(*v1.PersistentVolume))
   184→		},
   185→		UpdateFunc: func(_, obj interface{}) {
   186→			syncPV(obj.(*v1.PersistentVolume))
   187→		},
   188→	})
   189→}

   191→// CreateGCController creates a garbage controller and returns a run function
   192→// for it. The informer factory needs to be started before invoking that
   193→// function.
   194→func CreateGCController(ctx context.Context, tb ktesting.TB, restConfig restclient.Config, informerSet informers.SharedInformerFactory) func() {
   195→	restclient.AddUserAgent(&restConfig, "gc-controller")
   196→	clientSet := clientset.NewForConfigOrDie(&restConfig)
   197→	metadataClient, err := metadata.NewForConfig(&restConfig)
   198→	if err != nil {
   199→		tb.Fatalf("Failed to create metadataClient: %v", err)
   200→	}
   201→	restMapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(clientSet.Discovery()))
   202→	restMapper.Reset()
   203→	metadataInformers := metadatainformer.NewSharedInformerFactory(metadataClient, 0)
   204→	alwaysStarted := make(chan struct{})
   205→	close(alwaysStarted)
   206→	gc, err := garbagecollector.NewGarbageCollector(
   207→		ctx,
   208→		clientSet,
   209→		metadataClient,
   210→		restMapper,
   211→		garbagecollector.DefaultIgnoredResources(),
   212→		informerfactory.NewInformerFactory(informerSet, metadataInformers),
   213→		alwaysStarted,
   214→	)
   215→	if err != nil {
   216→		tb.Fatalf("Failed creating garbage collector")
   217→	}
   218→	startGC := func() {
   219→		syncPeriod := 5 * time.Second
   220→		go wait.Until(func() {
   221→			restMapper.Reset()
   222→		}, syncPeriod, ctx.Done())
   223→		go gc.Run(ctx, 1, syncPeriod)
   224→		go gc.Sync(ctx, clientSet.Discovery(), syncPeriod)
   225→	}
   226→	return startGC
   227→}
   228→
   229→// CreateNamespaceController creates a namespace controller and returns a run
   230→// function for it. The informer factory needs to be started before invoking
   231→// that function.
   232→func CreateNamespaceController(ctx context.Context, tb ktesting.TB, restConfig restclient.Config, informerSet informers.SharedInformerFactory) func() {
   233→	restclient.AddUserAgent(&restConfig, "namespace-controller")
   234→	clientSet := clientset.NewForConfigOrDie(&restConfig)
   235→	metadataClient, err := metadata.NewForConfig(&restConfig)
   236→	if err != nil {
   237→		tb.Fatalf("Failed to create metadataClient: %v", err)
   238→	}
   239→	discoverResourcesFn := clientSet.Discovery().ServerPreferredNamespacedResources
   240→	controller := namespace.NewNamespaceController(
   241→		ctx,
   242→		clientSet,
   243→		metadataClient,
   244→		discoverResourcesFn,
   245→		informerSet.Core().V1().Namespaces(),
   246→		10*time.Hour,
   247→		v1.FinalizerKubernetes)
   248→	return func() {
   249→		go controller.Run(ctx, 5)
   250→	}
   251→}

   253→// TestContext store necessary context info.
   254→// It also contains some optional parameters for InitTestScheduler.
   255→type TestContext struct {
   256→	// DisableEventSink, if set to true before calling InitTestScheduler,
   257→	// will skip the eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink and thus
   258→	// some extra goroutines which are tricky to get rid of after
   259→	// a test.
   260→	DisableEventSink bool
   261→
   262→	NS                 *v1.Namespace
   263→	ClientSet          clientset.Interface
   264→	KubeConfig         *restclient.Config
   265→	InformerFactory    informers.SharedInformerFactory
   266→	DynInformerFactory dynamicinformer.DynamicSharedInformerFactory
   267→	Scheduler          *scheduler.Scheduler
   268→	// This is the top context when initializing the test environment.
   269→	Ctx context.Context
   270→	// CloseFn will stop the apiserver and clean up the resources
   271→	// after itself, including shutting down its storage layer.
   272→	CloseFn framework.TearDownFunc
   273→	// This is the context when initializing scheduler.
   274→	SchedulerCtx context.Context
   275→	// SchedulerCloseFn will tear down the resources in creating scheduler,
   276→	// including the scheduler itself.
   277→	SchedulerCloseFn framework.TearDownFunc
   278→
   279→	// RoundTrip, if set, will be called for every HTTP request going to the apiserver.
   280→	// It can be used for error injection.
   281→	RoundTrip atomic.Pointer[RoundTripWrapper]
   282→}
   283→
   284→type RoundTripWrapper func(http.RoundTripper, *http.Request) (*http.Response, error)
   285→
   286→type roundTripWrapper struct {
   287→	tc        *TestContext
   288→	transport http.RoundTripper
   289→}
   290→
   291→func (r roundTripWrapper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
   292→	wrapper := r.tc.RoundTrip.Load()
   293→	if wrapper != nil {
   294→		return (*wrapper)(r.transport, req)
   295→	}
   296→	return r.transport.RoundTrip(req)
   297→}
   298→
   299→var _ http.RoundTripper = roundTripWrapper{}

   309→// PodDeleted returns true if a pod is not found in the given namespace.
   310→func PodDeleted(ctx context.Context, c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   311→	return func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   312→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   313→		if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
   314→			return true, nil
   315→		}
   316→		if pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil {
   317→			return true, nil
   318→		}
   319→		return false, nil
   320→	}
   321→}

   323→// PodsCleanedUp returns true if all pods are deleted in the specific namespace.
   324→func PodsCleanedUp(ctx context.Context, c clientset.Interface, namespace string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   325→	return func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   326→		list, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(namespace).List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
   327→		if err != nil {
   328→			return false, err
   329→		}
   330→		return len(list.Items) == 0, nil
   331→	}
   332→}

   334→// SyncSchedulerInformerFactory starts informer and waits for caches to be synced
   335→func SyncSchedulerInformerFactory(testCtx *TestContext) {
   336→	testCtx.InformerFactory.Start(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   337→	if testCtx.DynInformerFactory != nil {
   338→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory.Start(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   339→	}
   340→	testCtx.InformerFactory.WaitForCacheSync(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   341→	if testCtx.DynInformerFactory != nil {
   342→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory.WaitForCacheSync(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   343→	}
   344→}

   357→func RemovePodFinalizersInNamespace(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, t *testing.T, ns string) {
   358→	t.Helper()
   359→	pods, err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(ns).List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		t.Fatalf("Failed obtaining list of pods: %v", err)
   362→	}
   363→	RemovePodFinalizers(ctx, cs, t, pods.Items...)
   364→}
   365→
   366→// RemovePodFinalizers removes pod finalizers for the pods
   367→func RemovePodFinalizers(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, t *testing.T, pods ...v1.Pod) {
   368→	t.Helper()
   369→	for _, p := range pods {
   370→		pod, err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(p.Namespace).Get(ctx, p.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   371→		if err != nil && !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
   372→			t.Errorf("error while removing pod finalizers for %v: %v", klog.KObj(&p), err)
   373→		} else if pod != nil && len(pod.Finalizers) > 0 {
   374→			// Use Patch to remove finalizer, instead of Update, to avoid transient
   375→			// conflicts.
   376→			patchBytes, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]interface{}{
   377→				"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
   378→					"$deleteFromPrimitiveList/finalizers": pod.Finalizers,
   379→				},
   380→			})
   381→			_, err = cs.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Patch(ctx, pod.Name, types.StrategicMergePatchType, patchBytes, metav1.PatchOptions{})
   382→			if err != nil {
   383→				t.Errorf("error while updating pod status for %v: %v", klog.KObj(&p), err)
   384→			}
   385→		}
   386→	}
   387→}

   389→// CleanupPods deletes the given pods and waits for them to be actually deleted.
   390→func CleanupPods(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, t *testing.T, pods []*v1.Pod) {
   391→	for _, p := range pods {
   392→		err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(p.Namespace).Delete(ctx, p.Name, *metav1.NewDeleteOptions(0))
   393→		if err != nil && !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
   394→			t.Errorf("error while deleting pod %v/%v: %v", p.Namespace, p.Name, err)
   395→		}
   396→	}
   397→	for _, p := range pods {
   398→		if err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, time.Duration(time.Microsecond.Seconds()), wait.ForeverTestTimeout, true,
   399→			PodDeleted(ctx, cs, p.Namespace, p.Name)); err != nil {
   400→			t.Errorf("error while waiting for pod  %v/%v to get deleted: %v", p.Namespace, p.Name, err)
   401→		}
   402→	}
   403→}

   405→// AddTaintToNode add taints to specific node
   406→func AddTaintToNode(cs clientset.Interface, nodeName string, taint v1.Taint) error {
   407→	node, err := cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(context.TODO(), nodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		return err
   410→	}
   411→	node.Spec.Taints = append(node.Spec.Taints, taint)
   412→	_, err = cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Update(context.TODO(), node, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
   413→	return err
   414→}
   415→
   416→// RemoveTaintOffNode removes a specific taint from a node
   417→func RemoveTaintOffNode(cs clientset.Interface, nodeName string, taint v1.Taint) error {
   418→	node, err := cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(context.TODO(), nodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   419→	if err != nil {
   420→		return err
   421→	}
   422→	var taints []v1.Taint
   423→	for _, t := range node.Spec.Taints {
   424→		if !t.MatchTaint(&taint) {
   425→			taints = append(taints, t)
   426→		}
   427→	}
   428→	node.Spec.Taints = taints
   429→	_, err = cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Update(context.TODO(), node, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
   430→	return err
   431→}
   432→
   433→// WaitForNodeTaints waits for a node to have the target taints and returns
   434→// an error if it does not have taints within the given timeout.
   435→func WaitForNodeTaints(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, node *v1.Node, taints []v1.Taint) error {
   436→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, 30*time.Second, false, NodeTainted(ctx, cs, node.Name, taints))
   437→}
   438→
   439→// NodeTainted return a condition function that returns true if the given node contains
   440→// the taints.
   441→func NodeTainted(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, nodeName string, taints []v1.Taint) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   442→	return func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   443→		node, err := cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(ctx, nodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   444→		if err != nil {
   445→			return false, err
   446→		}
   447→
   448→		// node.Spec.Taints may have more taints
   449→		if len(taints) > len(node.Spec.Taints) {
   450→			return false, nil
   451→		}
   452→
   453→		for _, taint := range taints {
   454→			if !taintutils.TaintExists(node.Spec.Taints, &taint) {
   455→				return false, nil
   456→			}
   457→		}
   458→
   459→		return true, nil
   460→	}
   461→}

   463→// NodeReadyStatus returns the status of first condition with type NodeReady.
   464→// If none of the condition is of type NodeReady, returns an error.
   465→func NodeReadyStatus(conditions []v1.NodeCondition) (v1.ConditionStatus, error) {
   466→	for _, c := range conditions {
   467→		if c.Type != v1.NodeReady {
   468→			continue
   469→		}
   470→		// Just return the first condition with type NodeReady
   471→		return c.Status, nil
   472→	}
   473→	return v1.ConditionFalse, errors.New("None of the conditions is of type NodeReady")
   474→}
   475→
   476→// GetTolerationSeconds gets the period of time the toleration
   477→func GetTolerationSeconds(tolerations []v1.Toleration) (int64, error) {
   478→	for _, t := range tolerations {
   479→		if t.Key == v1.TaintNodeNotReady && t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute && t.Operator == v1.TolerationOpExists {
   480→			return *t.TolerationSeconds, nil
   481→		}
   482→	}
   483→	return 0, fmt.Errorf("cannot find toleration")
   484→}

   486→// NodeCopyWithConditions duplicates the ode object with conditions
   487→func NodeCopyWithConditions(node *v1.Node, conditions []v1.NodeCondition) *v1.Node {
   488→	copy := node.DeepCopy()
   489→	copy.ResourceVersion = "0"
   490→	copy.Status.Conditions = conditions
   491→	for i := range copy.Status.Conditions {
   492→		copy.Status.Conditions[i].LastHeartbeatTime = metav1.Now()
   493→	}
   494→	return copy
   495→}

   503→// InitTestAPIServer initializes a test environment and creates an API server with default
   504→// configuration.
   505→// It registers cleanup functions to t.Cleanup(), they will be called when the test completes,
   506→// no need to do this again.
   507→func InitTestAPIServer(t *testing.T, nsPrefix string, admission admission.Interface) *TestContext {
   508→	tCtx := ktesting.Init(t)
   509→	testCtx := &TestContext{Ctx: tCtx}
   510→
   511→	testCtx.ClientSet, testCtx.KubeConfig, testCtx.CloseFn = framework.StartTestServer(tCtx, t, framework.TestServerSetup{
   512→		ModifyServerRunOptions: func(options *options.ServerRunOptions) {
   513→			options.Admission.GenericAdmission.DisablePlugins = []string{"ServiceAccount", "TaintNodesByCondition", "Priority", "StorageObjectInUseProtection"}
   514→			if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.DynamicResourceAllocation) {
   515→				options.APIEnablement.RuntimeConfig = cliflag.ConfigurationMap{
   516→					resourceapi.SchemeGroupVersion.String(): "true",
   517→				}
   518→				if utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate.EmulationVersion().LessThan(version.MustParse("v1.34.0")) {
   519→					// Cannot enable the resourceapi.SchemeGroupVersion when emulating < 1.34 unless
   520→					// we enable --runtime-config-emulation-forward-compatible.
   521→					options.GenericServerRunOptions.RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible = true
   522→				}
   523→			}
   524→			if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.GenericWorkload) {
   525→				options.APIEnablement.RuntimeConfig = cliflag.ConfigurationMap{
   526→					schedulingapiv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(): "true",
   527→				}
   528→			}
   529→		},
   530→		ModifyServerConfig: func(config *controlplane.Config) {
   531→			if admission != nil {
   532→				config.ControlPlane.Generic.AdmissionControl = admission
   533→			}
   534→		},
   535→	})
   536→
   537→	// Support wrapping HTTP requests.
   538→	testCtx.KubeConfig.Wrap(func(transport http.RoundTripper) http.RoundTripper {
   539→		return roundTripWrapper{tc: testCtx, transport: transport}
   540→	})
   541→	var err error
   542→	testCtx.ClientSet, err = clientset.NewForConfig(testCtx.KubeConfig)
   543→	if err != nil {
   544→		t.Fatal(err)
   545→	}
   546→
   547→	oldCloseFn := testCtx.CloseFn
   548→	testCtx.CloseFn = func() {
   549→		tCtx.Cancel("tearing down apiserver")
   550→		oldCloseFn()
   551→	}
   552→
   553→	if nsPrefix != "default" {
   554→		testCtx.NS = framework.CreateNamespaceOrDie(testCtx.ClientSet, nsPrefix+string(uuid.NewUUID()), t)
   555→	} else {
   556→		testCtx.NS = framework.CreateNamespaceOrDie(testCtx.ClientSet, "default", t)
   557→	}
   558→
   559→	t.Cleanup(func() {
   560→		CleanupTest(t, testCtx)
   561→	})
   562→
   563→	return testCtx
   564→}

   566→// WaitForSchedulerCacheCleanup waits for cleanup of scheduler's cache to complete
   567→func WaitForSchedulerCacheCleanup(ctx context.Context, sched *scheduler.Scheduler, t *testing.T) {
   568→	schedulerCacheIsEmpty := func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   569→		dump := sched.Cache.Dump()
   570→
   571→		return len(dump.Nodes) == 0 && len(dump.AssumedPods) == 0, nil
   572→	}
   573→
   574→	if err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, time.Second, wait.ForeverTestTimeout, false, schedulerCacheIsEmpty); err != nil {
   575→		t.Errorf("Failed to wait for scheduler cache cleanup: %v", err)
   576→	}
   577→}

   579→// InitTestScheduler initializes a test environment and creates a scheduler with default
   580→// configuration.
   581→func InitTestScheduler(
   582→	t *testing.T,
   583→	testCtx *TestContext,
   584→) *TestContext {
   585→	// Pod preemption is enabled by default scheduler configuration.
   586→	return InitTestSchedulerWithOptions(t, testCtx, 0)
   587→}
   588→
   589→// InitTestSchedulerWithOptions initializes a test environment and creates a scheduler with default
   590→// configuration and other options.
   591→func InitTestSchedulerWithOptions(
   592→	t *testing.T,
   593→	testCtx *TestContext,
   594→	resyncPeriod time.Duration,
   595→	opts ...scheduler.Option,
   596→) *TestContext {
   597→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(testCtx.Ctx)
   598→	testCtx.SchedulerCtx = ctx
   599→
   600→	// 1. Create scheduler
   601→	testCtx.InformerFactory = scheduler.NewInformerFactory(testCtx.ClientSet, resyncPeriod)
   602→	if testCtx.KubeConfig != nil {
   603→		dynClient := dynamic.NewForConfigOrDie(testCtx.KubeConfig)
   604→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory = dynamicinformer.NewFilteredDynamicSharedInformerFactory(dynClient, 0, v1.NamespaceAll, nil)
   605→	}
   606→
   607→	var err error
   608→	eventBroadcaster := events.NewBroadcaster(&events.EventSinkImpl{
   609→		Interface: testCtx.ClientSet.EventsV1(),
   610→	})
   611→
   612→	opts = append(opts, scheduler.WithKubeConfig(testCtx.KubeConfig))
   613→	testCtx.Scheduler, err = scheduler.New(
   614→		ctx,
   615→		testCtx.ClientSet,
   616→		testCtx.InformerFactory,
   617→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory,
   618→		profile.NewRecorderFactory(eventBroadcaster),
   619→		opts...,
   620→	)
   621→
   622→	if err != nil {
   623→		t.Fatalf("Couldn't create scheduler: %v", err)
   624→	}
   625→
   626→	if !testCtx.DisableEventSink {
   627→		eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(ctx.Done())
   628→	}
   629→
   630→	oldCloseFn := testCtx.CloseFn
   631→	testCtx.CloseFn = func() {
   632→		oldCloseFn()
   633→		eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()
   634→	}
   635→
   636→	testCtx.SchedulerCloseFn = func() {
   637→		cancel()
   638→		eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()
   639→	}
   640→
   641→	return testCtx
   642→}

   644→// WaitForPodToScheduleWithTimeout waits for a pod to get scheduled and returns
   645→// an error if it does not scheduled within the given timeout.
   646→func WaitForPodToScheduleWithTimeout(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod, timeout time.Duration) error {
   647→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, timeout, false, PodScheduled(cs, pod.Namespace, pod.Name))
   648→}
   649→
   650→// WaitForPodToSchedule waits for a pod to get scheduled and returns an error if
   651→// it does not get scheduled within the timeout duration (30 seconds).
   652→func WaitForPodToSchedule(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod) error {
   653→	return WaitForPodToScheduleWithTimeout(ctx, cs, pod, 30*time.Second)
   654→}
   655→
   656→// PodScheduled checks if the pod has been scheduled
   657→func PodScheduled(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   658→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
   659→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   660→		if err != nil {
   661→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
   662→			return false, nil
   663→		}
   664→		if pod.Spec.NodeName == "" {
   665→			return false, nil
   666→		}
   667→		return true, nil
   668→	}
   669→}

   671→// InitDisruptionController initializes and runs a Disruption Controller to properly
   672→// update PodDisuptionBudget objects.
   673→func InitDisruptionController(t *testing.T, testCtx *TestContext) *disruption.DisruptionController {
   674→	informers := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(testCtx.ClientSet, 12*time.Hour)
   675→
   676→	discoveryClient := cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(testCtx.ClientSet.Discovery())
   677→	mapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(discoveryClient)
   678→
   679→	config := restclient.CopyConfig(testCtx.KubeConfig)
   680→	scaleKindResolver := scale.NewDiscoveryScaleKindResolver(testCtx.ClientSet.Discovery())
   681→	scaleClient, err := scale.NewForConfig(config, mapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, scaleKindResolver)
   682→	if err != nil {
   683→		t.Fatalf("Error in create scaleClient: %v", err)
   684→	}
   685→
   686→	dc := disruption.NewDisruptionController(
   687→		testCtx.Ctx,
   688→		informers.Core().V1().Pods(),
   689→		informers.Policy().V1().PodDisruptionBudgets(),
   690→		informers.Core().V1().ReplicationControllers(),
   691→		informers.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets(),
   692→		informers.Apps().V1().Deployments(),
   693→		informers.Apps().V1().StatefulSets(),
   694→		testCtx.ClientSet,
   695→		mapper,
   696→		scaleClient,
   697→		testCtx.ClientSet.Discovery())
   698→
   699→	informers.Start(testCtx.Scheduler.StopEverything)
   700→	informers.WaitForCacheSync(testCtx.Scheduler.StopEverything)
   701→	go dc.Run(testCtx.Ctx)
   702→	return dc
   703→}

   718→		Profiles: []kubeschedulerconfigv1.KubeSchedulerProfile{{

   738→// WaitForReflection waits till the passFunc confirms that the object it expects
   739→// to see is in the store. Used to observe reflected events.
   740→func WaitForReflection(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T, nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister, key string,
   741→	passFunc func(n interface{}) bool) error {
   742→	var nodes []*v1.Node
   743→	err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, time.Millisecond*100, wait.ForeverTestTimeout, false, func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   744→		n, err := nodeLister.Get(key)
   745→
   746→		switch {
   747→		case err == nil && passFunc(n):
   748→			return true, nil
   749→		case apierrors.IsNotFound(err):
   750→			nodes = append(nodes, nil)
   751→		case err != nil:
   752→			t.Errorf("Unexpected error: %v", err)
   753→		default:
   754→			nodes = append(nodes, n)
   755→		}
   756→
   757→		return false, nil
   758→	})
   759→	if err != nil {
   760→		t.Logf("Logging consecutive node versions received from store:")
   761→		for i, n := range nodes {
   762→			t.Logf("%d: %#v", i, n)
   763→		}
   764→	}
   765→	return err
   766→}

   772→func CreateNode(cs clientset.Interface, node *v1.Node) (*v1.Node, error) {
   773→	return cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Create(context.TODO(), node, metav1.CreateOptions{})
   774→}
   775→
   776→func createNodes(cs clientset.Interface, prefix string, wrapper *st.NodeWrapper, numNodes int) ([]*v1.Node, error) {
   777→	nodes := make([]*v1.Node, numNodes)
   778→	for i := range numNodes {
   779→		nodeName := fmt.Sprintf("%v-%d", prefix, i)
   780→		node, err := CreateNode(cs, wrapper.Name(nodeName).Label("kubernetes.io/hostname", nodeName).Obj())
   781→		if err != nil {
   782→			return nodes[:], err
   783→		}
   784→		nodes[i] = node
   785→	}
   786→	return nodes[:], nil
   787→}
   788→
   789→// CreateAndWaitForNodesInCache calls createNodes(), and wait for the created
   790→// nodes to be present in scheduler cache.
   791→func CreateAndWaitForNodesInCache(testCtx *TestContext, prefix string, wrapper *st.NodeWrapper, numNodes int) ([]*v1.Node, error) {
   792→	existingNodes := testCtx.Scheduler.Cache.NodeCount()
   793→	nodes, err := createNodes(testCtx.ClientSet, prefix, wrapper, numNodes)
   794→	if err != nil {
   795→		return nodes, fmt.Errorf("cannot create nodes: %v", err)
   796→	}
   797→	return nodes, WaitForNodesInCache(testCtx.Ctx, testCtx.Scheduler, numNodes+existingNodes)
   798→}
   799→
   800→// WaitForNodesInCache ensures at least <nodeCount> nodes are present in scheduler cache
   801→// within 30 seconds; otherwise returns false.
   802→func WaitForNodesInCache(ctx context.Context, sched *scheduler.Scheduler, nodeCount int) error {
   803→	err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, wait.ForeverTestTimeout, false, func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   804→		return sched.Cache.NodeCount() >= nodeCount, nil
   805→	})
   806→	if err != nil {
   807→		return fmt.Errorf("cannot obtain available nodes in scheduler cache: %v", err)
   808→	}
   809→	return nil
   810→}

   812→type PausePodConfig struct {
   813→	Name                              string
   814→	Namespace                         string
   815→	Affinity                          *v1.Affinity
   816→	Annotations, Labels, NodeSelector map[string]string
   817→	Resources                         *v1.ResourceRequirements
   818→	Tolerations                       []v1.Toleration
   819→	NodeName                          string
   820→	SchedulerName                     string
   821→	Priority                          *int32
   822→	PreemptionPolicy                  *v1.PreemptionPolicy
   823→	PriorityClassName                 string
   824→	Volumes                           []v1.Volume
   825→	ContainerPorts                    []v1.ContainerPort
   826→	RestartableInitContainerPorts     []v1.ContainerPort
   827→	NonRestartableInitContainerPorts  []v1.ContainerPort
   828→}
   829→
   830→// InitPausePod initializes a pod API object from the given config. It is used
   831→// mainly in pod creation process.
   832→func InitPausePod(conf *PausePodConfig) *v1.Pod {
   833→	pod := &v1.Pod{
   834→		ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
   835→			Name:        conf.Name,
   836→			Namespace:   conf.Namespace,
   837→			Labels:      conf.Labels,
   838→			Annotations: conf.Annotations,
   839→		},
   840→		Spec: v1.PodSpec{
   841→			NodeSelector: conf.NodeSelector,
   842→			Affinity:     conf.Affinity,
   843→			Containers: []v1.Container{
   844→				{
   845→					Name:  conf.Name,
   846→					Image: imageutils.GetPauseImageName(),
   847→					Ports: conf.ContainerPorts,
   848→				},
   849→			},
   850→			Tolerations:       conf.Tolerations,
   851→			NodeName:          conf.NodeName,
   852→			SchedulerName:     conf.SchedulerName,
   853→			Priority:          conf.Priority,
   854→			PreemptionPolicy:  conf.PreemptionPolicy,
   855→			PriorityClassName: conf.PriorityClassName,
   856→			Volumes:           conf.Volumes,
   857→		},
   858→	}
   859→	if conf.Resources != nil {
   860→		pod.Spec.Containers[0].Resources = *conf.Resources
   861→	}
   862→	if conf.RestartableInitContainerPorts != nil {
   863→		var containerRestartPolicyAlways = v1.ContainerRestartPolicyAlways
   864→		pod.Spec.InitContainers = []v1.Container{
   865→			{
   866→				Name:          conf.Name + "-init",
   867→				Image:         imageutils.GetPauseImageName(),
   868→				RestartPolicy: &containerRestartPolicyAlways,
   869→				Ports:         conf.RestartableInitContainerPorts,
   870→			},
   871→		}
   872→	}
   873→	if conf.NonRestartableInitContainerPorts != nil {
   874→		pod.Spec.InitContainers = []v1.Container{
   875→			{
   876→				Name:  conf.Name + "-init",
   877→				Image: imageutils.GetPauseImageName(),
   878→				Ports: conf.NonRestartableInitContainerPorts,
   879→			},
   880→		}
   881→	}
   882→	return pod
   883→}

   891→// CreatePausePodWithResource creates a pod with "Pause" image and the given
   892→// resources and returns its pointer and error status. The resource list can be
   893→// nil.
   894→func CreatePausePodWithResource(cs clientset.Interface, podName string,
   895→	nsName string, res *v1.ResourceList) (*v1.Pod, error) {
   896→	var conf PausePodConfig
   897→	if res == nil {
   898→		conf = PausePodConfig{
   899→			Name:      podName,
   900→			Namespace: nsName,
   901→		}
   902→	} else {
   903→		conf = PausePodConfig{
   904→			Name:      podName,
   905→			Namespace: nsName,
   906→			Resources: &v1.ResourceRequirements{
   907→				Requests: *res,
   908→			},
   909→		}
   910→	}
   911→	return CreatePausePod(cs, InitPausePod(&conf))
   912→}

   936→// RunPausePod creates a pod with "Pause" image and the given config and waits
   937→// until it is scheduled. It returns its pointer and error status.
   938→func RunPausePod(cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod) (*v1.Pod, error) {
   939→	pod, err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Create(context.TODO(), pod, metav1.CreateOptions{})
   940→	if err != nil {
   941→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create pause pod: %v", err)
   942→	}
   943→	if err = WaitForPodToSchedule(context.TODO(), cs, pod); err != nil {
   944→		return pod, fmt.Errorf("Pod %v/%v didn't schedule successfully. Error: %v", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err)
   945→	}
   946→	if pod, err = cs.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Get(context.TODO(), pod.Name, metav1.GetOptions{}); err != nil {
   947→		return pod, fmt.Errorf("failed to get pod %v/%v info: %v", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err)
   948→	}
   949→	return pod, nil
   950→}

   989→// PodIsGettingEvicted returns true if the pod's deletion timestamp is set.
   990→func PodIsGettingEvicted(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   991→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
   992→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   993→		if err != nil {
   994→			return false, err
   995→		}
   996→		if pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil {
   997→			return true, nil
   998→		}
   999→		return false, nil
  1000→	}
  1001→}

  1003→// PodScheduledIn returns true if a given pod is placed onto one of the expected nodes.
  1004→func PodScheduledIn(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string, nodeNames []string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
  1005→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1006→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1007→		if err != nil {
  1008→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
  1009→			return false, nil
  1010→		}
  1011→		if pod.Spec.NodeName == "" {
  1012→			return false, nil
  1013→		}
  1014→		for _, nodeName := range nodeNames {
  1015→			if pod.Spec.NodeName == nodeName {
  1016→				return true, nil
  1017→			}
  1018→		}
  1019→		return false, nil
  1020→	}
  1021→}

  1023→// PodUnschedulable returns a condition function that returns true if the given pod
  1024→// gets unschedulable status of reason 'Unschedulable'.
  1025→func PodUnschedulable(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
  1026→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1027→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1028→		if err != nil {
  1029→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
  1030→			return false, nil
  1031→		}
  1032→		_, cond := podutil.GetPodCondition(&pod.Status, v1.PodScheduled)
  1033→		return cond != nil && cond.Status == v1.ConditionFalse &&
  1034→			cond.Reason == v1.PodReasonUnschedulable && pod.Spec.NodeName == "", nil
  1035→	}
  1036→}
  1037→
  1038→// PodSchedulingError returns a condition function that returns true if the given pod
  1039→// gets unschedulable status for reasons other than "Unschedulable". The scheduler
  1040→// records such reasons in case of error.
  1041→func PodSchedulingError(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
  1042→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1043→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1044→		if err != nil {
  1045→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
  1046→			return false, nil
  1047→		}
  1048→		_, cond := podutil.GetPodCondition(&pod.Status, v1.PodScheduled)
  1049→		return cond != nil && cond.Status == v1.ConditionFalse &&
  1050→			cond.Reason != v1.PodReasonUnschedulable, nil
  1051→	}
  1052→}
  1053→
  1054→// PodSchedulingGated returns a condition function that returns true if the given pod
  1055→// gets unschedulable status of reason 'SchedulingGated'.
  1056→func PodSchedulingGated(ctx context.Context, c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
  1057→	return func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1058→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1059→		if err != nil {
  1060→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
  1061→			return false, nil
  1062→		}
  1063→		_, cond := podutil.GetPodCondition(&pod.Status, v1.PodScheduled)
  1064→		return cond != nil && cond.Status == v1.ConditionFalse &&
  1065→			cond.Reason == v1.PodReasonSchedulingGated && pod.Spec.NodeName == "", nil
  1066→	}
  1067→}

  1069→// WaitForPodUnschedulableWithTimeout waits for a pod to fail scheduling and returns
  1070→// an error if it does not become unschedulable within the given timeout.

  1072→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, timeout, false, PodUnschedulable(cs, pod.Namespace, pod.Name))

  1075→// WaitForPodUnschedulable waits for a pod to fail scheduling and returns

  1087→// WaitForPDBsStable waits for PDBs to have "CurrentHealthy" status equal to
  1088→// the expected values.
  1089→func WaitForPDBsStable(testCtx *TestContext, pdbs []*policy.PodDisruptionBudget, pdbPodNum []int32) error {
  1090→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, time.Second, 60*time.Second, false, func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1091→		pdbList, err := testCtx.ClientSet.PolicyV1().PodDisruptionBudgets(testCtx.NS.Name).List(testCtx.Ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
  1092→		if err != nil {
  1093→			return false, err
  1094→		}
  1095→		if len(pdbList.Items) != len(pdbs) {
  1096→			return false, nil
  1097→		}
  1098→		for i, pdb := range pdbs {
  1099→			found := false
  1100→			for _, cpdb := range pdbList.Items {
  1101→				if pdb.Name == cpdb.Name && pdb.Namespace == cpdb.Namespace {
  1102→					found = true
  1103→					if cpdb.Status.CurrentHealthy != pdbPodNum[i] {
  1104→						return false, nil
  1105→					}
  1106→				}
  1107→			}
  1108→			if !found {
  1109→				return false, nil
  1110→			}
  1111→		}
  1112→		return true, nil
  1113→	})
  1114→}
  1115→
  1116→// WaitCachedPodsStable waits until scheduler cache has the given pods.
  1117→func WaitCachedPodsStable(testCtx *TestContext, pods []*v1.Pod) error {
  1118→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, time.Second, 30*time.Second, false, func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1119→		cachedPods, err := testCtx.Scheduler.Cache.PodCount()
  1120→		if err != nil {
  1121→			return false, err
  1122→		}
  1123→		if len(pods) != cachedPods {
  1124→			return false, nil
  1125→		}
  1126→		for _, p := range pods {
  1127→			actualPod, err1 := testCtx.ClientSet.CoreV1().Pods(p.Namespace).Get(testCtx.Ctx, p.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1128→			if err1 != nil {
  1129→				return false, err1
  1130→			}
  1131→			cachedPod, err2 := testCtx.Scheduler.Cache.GetPod(actualPod)
  1132→			if err2 != nil || cachedPod == nil {
  1133→				return false, err2
  1134→			}
  1135→		}
  1136→		return true, nil
  1137→	})
  1138→}

  1158→
  1159→// timeout returns a timeout error if the given `f` function doesn't
  1160→// complete within `d` duration; otherwise it returns nil.
  1161→func timeout(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration, f func()) error {
  1162→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, d)
  1163→	defer cancel()
  1164→
  1165→	done := make(chan struct{})
  1166→	go func() {
  1167→		f()
  1168→		close(done)
  1169→	}()
  1170→
  1171→	select {
  1172→	case <-done:
  1173→		return nil
  1174→	case <-ctx.Done():
  1175→		return ctx.Err()
  1176→	}
  1177→}
  1178→
  1179→// NextPodOrDie returns the next Pod in the scheduler queue.
  1180→// The operation needs to be completed within 5 seconds; otherwise the test gets aborted.
  1181→func NextPodOrDie(t *testing.T, testCtx *TestContext) *schedulerframework.QueuedPodInfo {
  1182→	t.Helper()
  1183→
  1184→	var podInfo *schedulerframework.QueuedPodInfo
  1185→	logger := klog.FromContext(testCtx.Ctx)
  1186→	// NextPod() is a blocking operation. Wrap it in timeout() to avoid relying on
  1187→	// default go testing timeout (10m) to abort.
  1188→	if err := timeout(testCtx.Ctx, time.Second*5, func() {
  1189→		podInfo, _ = testCtx.Scheduler.NextPod(logger)
  1190→	}); err != nil {
  1191→		t.Fatalf("Timed out waiting for the Pod to be popped: %v", err)
  1192→	}
  1193→	return podInfo
  1194→}
  1195→
  1196→func WaitForNominatedNodeNameWithTimeout(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod, timeout time.Duration) error {
  1197→	if err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, timeout, false, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1198→		pod, err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Get(ctx, pod.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1199→		if err != nil {
  1200→			return false, err
  1201→		}
  1202→		if len(pod.Status.NominatedNodeName) > 0 {
  1203→			return true, nil
  1204→		}
  1205→		return false, err
  1206→	}); err != nil {
  1207→		return fmt.Errorf(".status.nominatedNodeName of Pod %v/%v did not get set: %w", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err)
  1208→	}
  1209→	return nil
  1210→}
  1211→
  1212→func WaitForNominatedNodeName(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod) error {
  1213→	return WaitForNominatedNodeNameWithTimeout(ctx, cs, pod, wait.ForeverTestTimeout)
  1214→}

    85→// StartScheduler configures and starts a scheduler given a handle to the clientSet interface
    86→// and event broadcaster. It returns the running scheduler and podInformer. Background goroutines
    87→// will keep running until the context is canceled.
    88→func StartScheduler(tCtx ktesting.TContext, cfg *kubeschedulerconfig.KubeSchedulerConfiguration, outOfTreePluginRegistry frameworkruntime.Registry) (*scheduler.Scheduler, informers.SharedInformerFactory) {
    89→	clientSet := tCtx.Client()
    90→	informerFactory := scheduler.NewInformerFactory(clientSet, 0)
    91→	evtBroadcaster := events.NewBroadcaster(&events.EventSinkImpl{
    92→		Interface: clientSet.EventsV1()})
    93→	go func() {
    94→		<-tCtx.Done()
    95→		evtBroadcaster.Shutdown()
    96→	}()
    97→
    98→	evtBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(tCtx.Done())
    99→
   100→	logger := tCtx.Logger()
   101→
   102→	sched, err := scheduler.New(
   103→		tCtx,
   104→		clientSet,
   105→		informerFactory,
   106→		nil,
   107→		profile.NewRecorderFactory(evtBroadcaster),
   108→		scheduler.WithKubeConfig(tCtx.RESTConfig()),
   109→		scheduler.WithProfiles(cfg.Profiles...),
   110→		scheduler.WithPercentageOfNodesToScore(cfg.PercentageOfNodesToScore),
   111→		scheduler.WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds(cfg.PodMaxBackoffSeconds),
   112→		scheduler.WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds(cfg.PodInitialBackoffSeconds),
   113→		scheduler.WithExtenders(cfg.Extenders...),
   114→		scheduler.WithParallelism(cfg.Parallelism),
   115→		scheduler.WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(outOfTreePluginRegistry),
   116→	)
   117→	tCtx.ExpectNoError(err, "creating scheduler")
   118→
   119→	informerFactory.Start(tCtx.Done())
   120→	informerFactory.WaitForCacheSync(tCtx.Done())
   121→	err = sched.WaitForHandlersSync(tCtx)
   122→	tCtx.ExpectNoError(err, "waiting for handlers to sync")
   123→	logger.V(3).Info("Handlers synced")
   124→	go sched.Run(tCtx)
   125→
   126→	return sched, informerFactory
   127→}

   129→func CreateResourceClaimController(ctx context.Context, tb ktesting.TB, clientSet clientset.Interface, informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory) func() {
   130→	podInformer := informerFactory.Core().V1().Pods()
   131→	claimInformer := informerFactory.Resource().V1().ResourceClaims()
   132→	claimTemplateInformer := informerFactory.Resource().V1().ResourceClaimTemplates()
   133→	features := resourceclaim.Features{
   134→		AdminAccess:     true,
   135→		PrioritizedList: true,
   136→	}
   137→	claimController, err := resourceclaim.NewController(klog.FromContext(ctx), features, clientSet, podInformer, claimInformer, claimTemplateInformer)
   138→	if err != nil {
   139→		tb.Fatalf("Error creating claim controller: %v", err)
   140→	}
   141→	return func() {
   142→		go claimController.Run(ctx, 5 /* workers */)
   143→	}
   144→}

   146→// StartFakePVController is a simplified pv controller logic that sets PVC VolumeName and annotation for each PV binding.
   147→// TODO(mborsz): Use a real PV controller here.
   148→func StartFakePVController(ctx context.Context, clientSet clientset.Interface, informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory) {
   149→	pvInformer := informerFactory.Core().V1().PersistentVolumes()
   150→
   151→	syncPV := func(obj *v1.PersistentVolume) {
   152→		if obj.Spec.ClaimRef != nil {
   153→			claimRef := obj.Spec.ClaimRef
   154→			pvc, err := clientSet.CoreV1().PersistentVolumeClaims(claimRef.Namespace).Get(ctx, claimRef.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   155→			if err != nil {
   156→				// Note that the error can be anything, because components like
   157→				// apiserver are also shutting down at the same time, but this
   158→				// check is conservative and only ignores the "context canceled"
   159→				// error while shutting down.
   160→				if ctx.Err() == nil || !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   161→					klog.Errorf("error while getting %v/%v: %v", claimRef.Namespace, claimRef.Name, err)
   162→				}
   163→				return
   164→			}
   165→
   166→			if pvc.Spec.VolumeName == "" {
   167→				pvc.Spec.VolumeName = obj.Name
   168→				metav1.SetMetaDataAnnotation(&pvc.ObjectMeta, pvutil.AnnBindCompleted, "yes")
   169→				_, err := clientSet.CoreV1().PersistentVolumeClaims(claimRef.Namespace).Update(ctx, pvc, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
   170→				if err != nil {
   171→					if ctx.Err() == nil || !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   172→						// Shutting down, no need to record this.
   173→						klog.Errorf("error while updating %v/%v: %v", claimRef.Namespace, claimRef.Name, err)
   174→					}
   175→					return
   176→				}
   177→			}
   178→		}
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	pvInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
   182→		AddFunc: func(obj interface{}) {
   183→			syncPV(obj.(*v1.PersistentVolume))
   184→		},
   185→		UpdateFunc: func(_, obj interface{}) {
   186→			syncPV(obj.(*v1.PersistentVolume))
   187→		},
   188→	})
   189→}

   191→// CreateGCController creates a garbage controller and returns a run function
   192→// for it. The informer factory needs to be started before invoking that
   193→// function.
   194→func CreateGCController(ctx context.Context, tb ktesting.TB, restConfig restclient.Config, informerSet informers.SharedInformerFactory) func() {
   195→	restclient.AddUserAgent(&restConfig, "gc-controller")
   196→	clientSet := clientset.NewForConfigOrDie(&restConfig)
   197→	metadataClient, err := metadata.NewForConfig(&restConfig)
   198→	if err != nil {
   199→		tb.Fatalf("Failed to create metadataClient: %v", err)
   200→	}
   201→	restMapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(clientSet.Discovery()))
   202→	restMapper.Reset()
   203→	metadataInformers := metadatainformer.NewSharedInformerFactory(metadataClient, 0)
   204→	alwaysStarted := make(chan struct{})
   205→	close(alwaysStarted)
   206→	gc, err := garbagecollector.NewGarbageCollector(
   207→		ctx,
   208→		clientSet,
   209→		metadataClient,
   210→		restMapper,
   211→		garbagecollector.DefaultIgnoredResources(),
   212→		informerfactory.NewInformerFactory(informerSet, metadataInformers),
   213→		alwaysStarted,
   214→	)
   215→	if err != nil {
   216→		tb.Fatalf("Failed creating garbage collector")
   217→	}
   218→	startGC := func() {
   219→		syncPeriod := 5 * time.Second
   220→		go wait.Until(func() {
   221→			restMapper.Reset()
   222→		}, syncPeriod, ctx.Done())
   223→		go gc.Run(ctx, 1, syncPeriod)
   224→		go gc.Sync(ctx, clientSet.Discovery(), syncPeriod)
   225→	}
   226→	return startGC
   227→}

   229→// CreateNamespaceController creates a namespace controller and returns a run
   230→// function for it. The informer factory needs to be started before invoking
   231→// that function.
   232→func CreateNamespaceController(ctx context.Context, tb ktesting.TB, restConfig restclient.Config, informerSet informers.SharedInformerFactory) func() {
   233→	restclient.AddUserAgent(&restConfig, "namespace-controller")
   234→	clientSet := clientset.NewForConfigOrDie(&restConfig)
   235→	metadataClient, err := metadata.NewForConfig(&restConfig)
   236→	if err != nil {
   237→		tb.Fatalf("Failed to create metadataClient: %v", err)
   238→	}
   239→	discoverResourcesFn := clientSet.Discovery().ServerPreferredNamespacedResources
   240→	controller := namespace.NewNamespaceController(
   241→		ctx,
   242→		clientSet,
   243→		metadataClient,
   244→		discoverResourcesFn,
   245→		informerSet.Core().V1().Namespaces(),
   246→		10*time.Hour,
   247→		v1.FinalizerKubernetes)
   248→	return func() {
   249→		go controller.Run(ctx, 5)
   250→	}
   251→}

   253→// TestContext store necessary context info.
   254→// It also contains some optional parameters for InitTestScheduler.
   255→type TestContext struct {
   256→	// DisableEventSink, if set to true before calling InitTestScheduler,
   257→	// will skip the eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink and thus
   258→	// some extra goroutines which are tricky to get rid of after
   259→	// a test.
   260→	DisableEventSink bool
   261→
   262→	NS                 *v1.Namespace
   263→	ClientSet          clientset.Interface
   264→	KubeConfig         *restclient.Config
   265→	InformerFactory    informers.SharedInformerFactory
   266→	DynInformerFactory dynamicinformer.DynamicSharedInformerFactory
   267→	Scheduler          *scheduler.Scheduler
   268→	// This is the top context when initializing the test environment.
   269→	Ctx context.Context
   270→	// CloseFn will stop the apiserver and clean up the resources
   271→	// after itself, including shutting down its storage layer.
   272→	CloseFn framework.TearDownFunc
   273→	// This is the context when initializing scheduler.
   274→	SchedulerCtx context.Context
   275→	// SchedulerCloseFn will tear down the resources in creating scheduler,
   276→	// including the scheduler itself.
   277→	SchedulerCloseFn framework.TearDownFunc
   278→
   279→	// RoundTrip, if set, will be called for every HTTP request going to the apiserver.
   280→	// It can be used for error injection.
   281→	RoundTrip atomic.Pointer[RoundTripWrapper]
   282→}
   283→
   284→type RoundTripWrapper func(http.RoundTripper, *http.Request) (*http.Response, error)
   285→
   286→type roundTripWrapper struct {
   287→	tc        *TestContext
   288→	transport http.RoundTripper
   289→}
   290→
   291→func (r roundTripWrapper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
   292→	wrapper := r.tc.RoundTrip.Load()
   293→	if wrapper != nil {
   294→		return (*wrapper)(r.transport, req)
   295→	}
   296→	return r.transport.RoundTrip(req)
   297→}
   298→
   299→var _ http.RoundTripper = roundTripWrapper{}

   309→// PodDeleted returns true if a pod is not found in the given namespace.
   310→func PodDeleted(ctx context.Context, c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   311→	return func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   312→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   313→		if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
   314→			return true, nil
   315→		}
   316→		if pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil {
   317→			return true, nil
   318→		}
   319→		return false, nil
   320→	}
   321→}
   322→
   323→// PodsCleanedUp returns true if all pods are deleted in the specific namespace.
   324→func PodsCleanedUp(ctx context.Context, c clientset.Interface, namespace string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   325→	return func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   326→		list, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(namespace).List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
   327→		if err != nil {
   328→			return false, err
   329→		}
   330→		return len(list.Items) == 0, nil
   331→	}
   332→}

   334→// SyncSchedulerInformerFactory starts informer and waits for caches to be synced
   335→func SyncSchedulerInformerFactory(testCtx *TestContext) {
   336→	testCtx.InformerFactory.Start(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   337→	if testCtx.DynInformerFactory != nil {
   338→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory.Start(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   339→	}
   340→	testCtx.InformerFactory.WaitForCacheSync(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   341→	if testCtx.DynInformerFactory != nil {
   342→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory.WaitForCacheSync(testCtx.SchedulerCtx.Done())
   343→	}
   344→}

   346→// CleanupTest cleans related resources which were created during integration test

   350→		t.Errorf("error while cleaning up nodes, error: %v", err)

   352→	framework.DeleteNamespaceOrDie(testCtx.ClientSet, testCtx.NS, t)

   357→func RemovePodFinalizersInNamespace(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, t *testing.T, ns string) {
   358→	t.Helper()
   359→	pods, err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(ns).List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
   360→	if err != nil {

   362→	}

   364→}

   366→// RemovePodFinalizers removes pod finalizers for the pods
   367→func RemovePodFinalizers(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, t *testing.T, pods ...v1.Pod) {
   368→	t.Helper()
   369→	for _, p := range pods {
   370→		pod, err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(p.Namespace).Get(ctx, p.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   371→		if err != nil && !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
   372→			t.Errorf("error while removing pod finalizers for %v: %v", klog.KObj(&p), err)
   373→		} else if pod != nil && len(pod.Finalizers) > 0 {
   374→			// Use Patch to remove finalizer, instead of Update, to avoid transient
   375→			// conflicts.
   376→			patchBytes, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]interface{}{
   377→				"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
   378→					"$deleteFromPrimitiveList/finalizers": pod.Finalizers,
   379→				},
   380→			})
   381→			_, err = cs.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Patch(ctx, pod.Name, types.StrategicMergePatchType, patchBytes, metav1.PatchOptions{})
   382→			if err != nil {
   383→				t.Errorf("error while updating pod status for %v: %v", klog.KObj(&p), err)
   384→			}
   385→		}
   386→	}
   387→}

   389→// CleanupPods deletes the given pods and waits for them to be actually deleted.
   390→func CleanupPods(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, t *testing.T, pods []*v1.Pod) {
   391→	for _, p := range pods {
   392→		err := cs.CoreV1().Pods(p.Namespace).Delete(ctx, p.Name, *metav1.NewDeleteOptions(0))
   393→		if err != nil && !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
   394→			t.Errorf("error while deleting pod %v/%v: %v", p.Namespace, p.Name, err)
   395→		}
   396→	}
   397→	for _, p := range pods {
   398→		if err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, time.Duration(time.Microsecond.Seconds()), wait.ForeverTestTimeout, true,
   399→			PodDeleted(ctx, cs, p.Namespace, p.Name)); err != nil {
   400→			t.Errorf("error while waiting for pod  %v/%v to get deleted: %v", p.Namespace, p.Name, err)
   401→		}
   402→	}
   403→}

   405→// AddTaintToNode add taints to specific node
   406→func AddTaintToNode(cs clientset.Interface, nodeName string, taint v1.Taint) error {
   407→	node, err := cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(context.TODO(), nodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		return err
   410→	}
   411→	node.Spec.Taints = append(node.Spec.Taints, taint)
   412→	_, err = cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Update(context.TODO(), node, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
   413→	return err
   414→}
   415→
   416→// RemoveTaintOffNode removes a specific taint from a node
   417→func RemoveTaintOffNode(cs clientset.Interface, nodeName string, taint v1.Taint) error {
   418→	node, err := cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(context.TODO(), nodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   419→	if err != nil {
   420→		return err
   421→	}
   422→	var taints []v1.Taint
   423→	for _, t := range node.Spec.Taints {
   424→		if !t.MatchTaint(&taint) {
   425→			taints = append(taints, t)
   426→		}
   427→	}
   428→	node.Spec.Taints = taints
   429→	_, err = cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Update(context.TODO(), node, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
   430→	return err
   431→}

   433→// WaitForNodeTaints waits for a node to have the target taints and returns
   434→// an error if it does not have taints within the given timeout.
   435→func WaitForNodeTaints(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, node *v1.Node, taints []v1.Taint) error {
   436→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, 30*time.Second, false, NodeTainted(ctx, cs, node.Name, taints))
   437→}
   438→
   439→// NodeTainted return a condition function that returns true if the given node contains
   440→// the taints.
   441→func NodeTainted(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, nodeName string, taints []v1.Taint) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   442→	return func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   443→		node, err := cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(ctx, nodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   444→		if err != nil {
   445→			return false, err
   446→		}
   447→
   448→		// node.Spec.Taints may have more taints
   449→		if len(taints) > len(node.Spec.Taints) {
   450→			return false, nil
   451→		}
   452→
   453→		for _, taint := range taints {
   454→			if !taintutils.TaintExists(node.Spec.Taints, &taint) {
   455→				return false, nil
   456→			}
   457→		}
   458→
   459→		return true, nil
   460→	}
   461→}
   462→
   463→// NodeReadyStatus returns the status of first condition with type NodeReady.
   464→// If none of the condition is of type NodeReady, returns an error.
   465→func NodeReadyStatus(conditions []v1.NodeCondition) (v1.ConditionStatus, error) {
   466→	for _, c := range conditions {
   467→		if c.Type != v1.NodeReady {
   468→			continue
   469→		}
   470→		// Just return the first condition with type NodeReady
   471→		return c.Status, nil
   472→	}
   473→	return v1.ConditionFalse, errors.New("None of the conditions is of type NodeReady")
   474→}
   475→
   476→// GetTolerationSeconds gets the period of time the toleration
   477→func GetTolerationSeconds(tolerations []v1.Toleration) (int64, error) {
   478→	for _, t := range tolerations {
   479→		if t.Key == v1.TaintNodeNotReady && t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute && t.Operator == v1.TolerationOpExists {
   480→			return *t.TolerationSeconds, nil
   481→		}
   482→	}
   483→	return 0, fmt.Errorf("cannot find toleration")
   484→}
   485→
   486→// NodeCopyWithConditions duplicates the ode object with conditions
   487→func NodeCopyWithConditions(node *v1.Node, conditions []v1.NodeCondition) *v1.Node {
   488→	copy := node.DeepCopy()
   489→	copy.ResourceVersion = "0"
   490→	copy.Status.Conditions = conditions
   491→	for i := range copy.Status.Conditions {
   492→		copy.Status.Conditions[i].LastHeartbeatTime = metav1.Now()
   493→	}
   494→	return copy
   495→}

   499→	_, err := cs.CoreV1().Nodes().UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), node, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
   500→	return err
   501→}

   503→// InitTestAPIServer initializes a test environment and creates an API server with default
   504→// configuration.
   505→// It registers cleanup functions to t.Cleanup(), they will be called when the test completes,
   506→// no need to do this again.
   507→func InitTestAPIServer(t *testing.T, nsPrefix string, admission admission.Interface) *TestContext {
   508→	tCtx := ktesting.Init(t)
   509→	testCtx := &TestContext{Ctx: tCtx}
   510→
   511→	testCtx.ClientSet, testCtx.KubeConfig, testCtx.CloseFn = framework.StartTestServer(tCtx, t, framework.TestServerSetup{
   512→		ModifyServerRunOptions: func(options *options.ServerRunOptions) {
   513→			options.Admission.GenericAdmission.DisablePlugins = []string{"ServiceAccount", "TaintNodesByCondition", "Priority", "StorageObjectInUseProtection"}
   514→			if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.DynamicResourceAllocation) {
   515→				options.APIEnablement.RuntimeConfig = cliflag.ConfigurationMap{
   516→					resourceapi.SchemeGroupVersion.String(): "true",
   517→				}
   518→				if utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate.EmulationVersion().LessThan(version.MustParse("v1.34.0")) {
   519→					// Cannot enable the resourceapi.SchemeGroupVersion when emulating < 1.34 unless
   520→					// we enable --runtime-config-emulation-forward-compatible.
   521→					options.GenericServerRunOptions.RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible = true
   522→				}
   523→			}
   524→			if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.GenericWorkload) {
   525→				options.APIEnablement.RuntimeConfig = cliflag.ConfigurationMap{
   526→					schedulingapiv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(): "true",
   527→				}
   528→			}
   529→		},
   530→		ModifyServerConfig: func(config *controlplane.Config) {
   531→			if admission != nil {
   532→				config.ControlPlane.Generic.AdmissionControl = admission
   533→			}
   534→		},
   535→	})
   536→
   537→	// Support wrapping HTTP requests.
   538→	testCtx.KubeConfig.Wrap(func(transport http.RoundTripper) http.RoundTripper {
   539→		return roundTripWrapper{tc: testCtx, transport: transport}
   540→	})
   541→	var err error
   542→	testCtx.ClientSet, err = clientset.NewForConfig(testCtx.KubeConfig)
   543→	if err != nil {
   544→		t.Fatal(err)
   545→	}
   546→
   547→	oldCloseFn := testCtx.CloseFn
   548→	testCtx.CloseFn = func() {
   549→		tCtx.Cancel("tearing down apiserver")
   550→		oldCloseFn()
   551→	}
   552→
   553→	if nsPrefix != "default" {
   554→		testCtx.NS = framework.CreateNamespaceOrDie(testCtx.ClientSet, nsPrefix+string(uuid.NewUUID()), t)
   555→	} else {
   556→		testCtx.NS = framework.CreateNamespaceOrDie(testCtx.ClientSet, "default", t)
   557→	}
   558→
   559→	t.Cleanup(func() {
   560→		CleanupTest(t, testCtx)
   561→	})
   562→
   563→	return testCtx
   564→}

   566→// WaitForSchedulerCacheCleanup waits for cleanup of scheduler's cache to complete
   567→func WaitForSchedulerCacheCleanup(ctx context.Context, sched *scheduler.Scheduler, t *testing.T) {
   568→	schedulerCacheIsEmpty := func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   569→		dump := sched.Cache.Dump()
   570→
   571→		return len(dump.Nodes) == 0 && len(dump.AssumedPods) == 0, nil
   572→	}
   573→
   574→	if err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, time.Second, wait.ForeverTestTimeout, false, schedulerCacheIsEmpty); err != nil {
   575→		t.Errorf("Failed to wait for scheduler cache cleanup: %v", err)
   576→	}
   577→}

   579→// InitTestScheduler initializes a test environment and creates a scheduler with default
   580→// configuration.
   581→func InitTestScheduler(
   582→	t *testing.T,
   583→	testCtx *TestContext,
   584→) *TestContext {
   585→	// Pod preemption is enabled by default scheduler configuration.
   586→	return InitTestSchedulerWithOptions(t, testCtx, 0)
   587→}
   588→
   589→// InitTestSchedulerWithOptions initializes a test environment and creates a scheduler with default
   590→// configuration and other options.
   591→func InitTestSchedulerWithOptions(
   592→	t *testing.T,
   593→	testCtx *TestContext,
   594→	resyncPeriod time.Duration,
   595→	opts ...scheduler.Option,
   596→) *TestContext {
   597→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(testCtx.Ctx)
   598→	testCtx.SchedulerCtx = ctx
   599→
   600→	// 1. Create scheduler
   601→	testCtx.InformerFactory = scheduler.NewInformerFactory(testCtx.ClientSet, resyncPeriod)
   602→	if testCtx.KubeConfig != nil {
   603→		dynClient := dynamic.NewForConfigOrDie(testCtx.KubeConfig)
   604→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory = dynamicinformer.NewFilteredDynamicSharedInformerFactory(dynClient, 0, v1.NamespaceAll, nil)
   605→	}
   606→
   607→	var err error
   608→	eventBroadcaster := events.NewBroadcaster(&events.EventSinkImpl{
   609→		Interface: testCtx.ClientSet.EventsV1(),
   610→	})
   611→
   612→	opts = append(opts, scheduler.WithKubeConfig(testCtx.KubeConfig))
   613→	testCtx.Scheduler, err = scheduler.New(
   614→		ctx,
   615→		testCtx.ClientSet,
   616→		testCtx.InformerFactory,
   617→		testCtx.DynInformerFactory,
   618→		profile.NewRecorderFactory(eventBroadcaster),
   619→		opts...,
   620→	)
   621→
   622→	if err != nil {
   623→		t.Fatalf("Couldn't create scheduler: %v", err)
   624→	}
   625→
   626→	if !testCtx.DisableEventSink {
   627→		eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(ctx.Done())
   628→	}
   629→
   630→	oldCloseFn := testCtx.CloseFn
   631→	testCtx.CloseFn = func() {
   632→		oldCloseFn()
   633→		eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()
   634→	}
   635→
   636→	testCtx.SchedulerCloseFn = func() {
   637→		cancel()
   638→		eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()
   639→	}
   640→
   641→	return testCtx
   642→}

   644→// WaitForPodToScheduleWithTimeout waits for a pod to get scheduled and returns
   645→// an error if it does not scheduled within the given timeout.
   646→func WaitForPodToScheduleWithTimeout(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod, timeout time.Duration) error {
   647→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, timeout, false, PodScheduled(cs, pod.Namespace, pod.Name))
   648→}
   649→
   650→// WaitForPodToSchedule waits for a pod to get scheduled and returns an error if
   651→// it does not get scheduled within the timeout duration (30 seconds).
   652→func WaitForPodToSchedule(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, pod *v1.Pod) error {
   653→	return WaitForPodToScheduleWithTimeout(ctx, cs, pod, 30*time.Second)
   654→}
   655→
   656→// PodScheduled checks if the pod has been scheduled
   657→func PodScheduled(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
   658→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
   659→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   660→		if err != nil {
   661→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
   662→			return false, nil
   663→		}
   664→		if pod.Spec.NodeName == "" {
   665→			return false, nil
   666→		}
   667→		return true, nil
   668→	}
   669→}

   671→// InitDisruptionController initializes and runs a Disruption Controller to properly
   672→// update PodDisuptionBudget objects.
   673→func InitDisruptionController(t *testing.T, testCtx *TestContext) *disruption.DisruptionController {
   674→	informers := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(testCtx.ClientSet, 12*time.Hour)
   675→
   676→	discoveryClient := cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(testCtx.ClientSet.Discovery())
   677→	mapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(discoveryClient)
   678→
   679→	config := restclient.CopyConfig(testCtx.KubeConfig)
   680→	scaleKindResolver := scale.NewDiscoveryScaleKindResolver(testCtx.ClientSet.Discovery())
   681→	scaleClient, err := scale.NewForConfig(config, mapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, scaleKindResolver)
   682→	if err != nil {
   683→		t.Fatalf("Error in create scaleClient: %v", err)
   684→	}
   685→
   686→	dc := disruption.NewDisruptionController(
   687→		testCtx.Ctx,
   688→		informers.Core().V1().Pods(),
   689→		informers.Policy().V1().PodDisruptionBudgets(),
   690→		informers.Core().V1().ReplicationControllers(),
   691→		informers.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets(),
   692→		informers.Apps().V1().Deployments(),
   693→		informers.Apps().V1().StatefulSets(),
   694→		testCtx.ClientSet,
   695→		mapper,
   696→		scaleClient,
   697→		testCtx.ClientSet.Discovery())
   698→
   699→	informers.Start(testCtx.Scheduler.StopEverything)
   700→	informers.WaitForCacheSync(testCtx.Scheduler.StopEverything)
   701→	go dc.Run(testCtx.Ctx)
   702→	return dc
   703→}

   738→// WaitForReflection waits till the passFunc confirms that the object it expects
   739→// to see is in the store. Used to observe reflected events.
   740→func WaitForReflection(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T, nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister, key string,
   741→	passFunc func(n interface{}) bool) error {
   742→	var nodes []*v1.Node
   743→	err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, time.Millisecond*100, wait.ForeverTestTimeout, false, func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   744→		n, err := nodeLister.Get(key)
   745→
   746→		switch {
   747→		case err == nil && passFunc(n):
   748→			return true, nil
   749→		case apierrors.IsNotFound(err):
   750→			nodes = append(nodes, nil)
   751→		case err != nil:
   752→			t.Errorf("Unexpected error: %v", err)
   753→		default:
   754→			nodes = append(nodes, n)
   755→		}
   756→
   757→		return false, nil
   758→	})
   759→	if err != nil {
   760→		t.Logf("Logging consecutive node versions received from store:")
   761→		for i, n := range nodes {
   762→			t.Logf("%d: %#v", i, n)
   763→		}
   764→	}
   765→	return err
   766→}

   770→}
   771→
   772→func CreateNode(cs clientset.Interface, node *v1.Node) (*v1.Node, error) {
   773→	return cs.CoreV1().Nodes().Create(context.TODO(), node, metav1.CreateOptions{})
   774→}
   775→
   776→func createNodes(cs clientset.Interface, prefix string, wrapper *st.NodeWrapper, numNodes int) ([]*v1.Node, error) {
   777→	nodes := make([]*v1.Node, numNodes)
   778→	for i := range numNodes {
   779→		nodeName := fmt.Sprintf("%v-%d", prefix, i)
   780→		node, err := CreateNode(cs, wrapper.Name(nodeName).Label("kubernetes.io/hostname", nodeName).Obj())
   781→		if err != nil {
   782→			return nodes[:], err
   783→		}
   784→		nodes[i] = node
   785→	}
   786→	return nodes[:], nil
   787→}
   788→
   789→// CreateAndWaitForNodesInCache calls createNodes(), and wait for the created
   790→// nodes to be present in scheduler cache.
   791→func CreateAndWaitForNodesInCache(testCtx *TestContext, prefix string, wrapper *st.NodeWrapper, numNodes int) ([]*v1.Node, error) {
   792→	existingNodes := testCtx.Scheduler.Cache.NodeCount()
   793→	nodes, err := createNodes(testCtx.ClientSet, prefix, wrapper, numNodes)
   794→	if err != nil {
   795→		return nodes, fmt.Errorf("cannot create nodes: %v", err)
   796→	}
   797→	return nodes, WaitForNodesInCache(testCtx.Ctx, testCtx.Scheduler, numNodes+existingNodes)
   798→}
   799→
   800→// WaitForNodesInCache ensures at least <nodeCount> nodes are present in scheduler cache
   801→// within 30 seconds; otherwise returns false.
   802→func WaitForNodesInCache(ctx context.Context, sched *scheduler.Scheduler, nodeCount int) error {
   803→	err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond, wait.ForeverTestTimeout, false, func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
   804→		return sched.Cache.NodeCount() >= nodeCount, nil
   805→	})
   806→	if err != nil {
   807→		return fmt.Errorf("cannot obtain available nodes in scheduler cache: %v", err)
   808→	}
   809→	return nil
   810→}

   812→type PausePodConfig struct {
   813→	Name                              string
   814→	Namespace                         string
   815→	Affinity                          *v1.Affinity
   816→	Annotations, Labels, NodeSelector map[string]string
   817→	Resources                         *v1.ResourceRequirements
   818→	Tolerations                       []v1.Toleration
   819→	NodeName                          string
   820→	SchedulerName                     string
   821→	Priority                          *int32
   822→	PreemptionPolicy                  *v1.PreemptionPolicy
   823→	PriorityClassName                 string
   824→	Volumes                           []v1.Volume
   825→	ContainerPorts                    []v1.ContainerPort
   826→	RestartableInitContainerPorts     []v1.ContainerPort
   827→	NonRestartableInitContainerPorts  []v1.ContainerPort
   828→}
   829→
   830→// InitPausePod initializes a pod API object from the given config. It is used
   831→// mainly in pod creation process.
   832→func InitPausePod(conf *PausePodConfig) *v1.Pod {
   833→	pod := &v1.Pod{
   834→		ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
   835→			Name:        conf.Name,
   836→			Namespace:   conf.Namespace,
   837→			Labels:      conf.Labels,
   838→			Annotations: conf.Annotations,
   839→		},
   840→		Spec: v1.PodSpec{
   841→			NodeSelector: conf.NodeSelector,
   842→			Affinity:     conf.Affinity,
   843→			Containers: []v1.Container{
   844→				{
   845→					Name:  conf.Name,
   846→					Image: imageutils.GetPauseImageName(),
   847→					Ports: conf.ContainerPorts,
   848→				},
   849→			},
   850→			Tolerations:       conf.Tolerations,
   851→			NodeName:          conf.NodeName,
   852→			SchedulerName:     conf.SchedulerName,
   853→			Priority:          conf.Priority,
   854→			PreemptionPolicy:  conf.PreemptionPolicy,
   855→			PriorityClassName: conf.PriorityClassName,
   856→			Volumes:           conf.Volumes,
   857→		},
   858→	}
   859→	if conf.Resources != nil {
   860→		pod.Spec.Containers[0].Resources = *conf.Resources
   861→	}
   862→	if conf.RestartableInitContainerPorts != nil {
   863→		var containerRestartPolicyAlways = v1.ContainerRestartPolicyAlways
   864→		pod.Spec.InitContainers = []v1.Container{
   865→			{
   866→				Name:          conf.Name + "-init",
   867→				Image:         imageutils.GetPauseImageName(),
   868→				RestartPolicy: &containerRestartPolicyAlways,
   869→				Ports:         conf.RestartableInitContainerPorts,
   870→			},
   871→		}
   872→	}
   873→	if conf.NonRestartableInitContainerPorts != nil {
   874→		pod.Spec.InitContainers = []v1.Container{
   875→			{
   876→				Name:  conf.Name + "-init",
   877→				Image: imageutils.GetPauseImageName(),
   878→				Ports: conf.NonRestartableInitContainerPorts,
   879→			},
   880→		}
   881→	}
   882→	return pod
   883→}

   885→// CreatePausePod creates a pod with "Pause" image and the given config and
   886→// return its pointer and error status.
   887→func CreatePausePod(cs clientset.Interface, p *v1.Pod) (*v1.Pod, error) {
   888→	return cs.CoreV1().Pods(p.Namespace).Create(context.TODO(), p, metav1.CreateOptions{})
   889→}
   890→
   891→// CreatePausePodWithResource creates a pod with "Pause" image and the given
   892→// resources and returns its pointer and error status. The resource list can be
   893→// nil.
   894→func CreatePausePodWithResource(cs clientset.Interface, podName string,
   895→	nsName string, res *v1.ResourceList) (*v1.Pod, error) {
   896→	var conf PausePodConfig
   897→	if res == nil {
   898→		conf = PausePodConfig{
   899→			Name:      podName,
   900→			Namespace: nsName,
   901→		}
   902→	} else {
   903→		conf = PausePodConfig{
   904→			Name:      podName,
   905→			Namespace: nsName,
   906→			Resources: &v1.ResourceRequirements{
   907→				Requests: *res,
   908→			},
   909→		}
   910→	}
   911→	return CreatePausePod(cs, InitPausePod(&conf))
   912→}

   945→	}
   946→	if pod, err = cs.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Get(context.TODO(), pod.Name, metav1.GetOptions{}); err != nil {
   947→		return pod, fmt.Errorf("failed to get pod %v/%v info: %v", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err)

   959→// pods with containers each having a specific image.

   961→	pod := &v1.Pod{

  1005→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1006→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1007→		if err != nil {
  1008→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
  1009→			return false, nil
  1010→		}
  1011→		if pod.Spec.NodeName == "" {
  1012→			return false, nil
  1013→		}
  1014→		for _, nodeName := range nodeNames {
  1015→			if pod.Spec.NodeName == nodeName {
  1016→				return true, nil
  1017→			}

  1019→		return false, nil
  1020→	}

  1023→// PodUnschedulable returns a condition function that returns true if the given pod
  1024→// gets unschedulable status of reason 'Unschedulable'.
  1025→func PodUnschedulable(c clientset.Interface, podNamespace, podName string) wait.ConditionWithContextFunc {
  1026→	return func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1027→		pod, err := c.CoreV1().Pods(podNamespace).Get(ctx, podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1028→		if err != nil {
  1029→			// This could be a connection error so we want to retry.
  1030→			return false, nil
  1031→		}
  1032→		_, cond := podutil.GetPodCondition(&pod.Status, v1.PodScheduled)
  1033→		return cond != nil && cond.Status == v1.ConditionFalse &&
  1034→			cond.Reason == v1.PodReasonUnschedulable && pod.Spec.NodeName == "", nil
  1035→	}

  1049→		return cond != nil && cond.Status == v1.ConditionFalse &&
  1050→			cond.Reason != v1.PodReasonUnschedulable, nil
