  1087→// WaitForPDBsStable waits for PDBs to have "CurrentHealthy" status equal to
  1088→// the expected values.
  1089→func WaitForPDBsStable(testCtx *TestContext, pdbs []*policy.PodDisruptionBudget, pdbPodNum []int32) error {
  1090→	return wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, time.Second, 60*time.Second, false, func(context.Context) (bool, error) {
  1091→		pdbList, err := testCtx.ClientSet.PolicyV1().PodDisruptionBudgets(testCtx.NS.Name).List(testCtx.Ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
  1092→		if err != nil {
  1093→			return false, err
  1094→		}
  1095→		if len(pdbList.Items) != len(pdbs) {
  1096→			return false, nil
  1097→		}
  1098→		for i, pdb := range pdbs {
  1099→			found := false
  1100→			for _, cpdb := range pdbList.Items {
  1101→				if pdb.Name == cpdb.Name && pdb.Namespace == cpdb.Namespace {
  1102→					found = true
  1103→					if cpdb.Status.CurrentHealthy != pdbPodNum[i] {
  1104→						return false, nil
  1105→					}
  1106→				}
  1107→			}
  1108→			if !found {
  1109→				return false, nil
  1110→			}
  1111→		}
  1112→		return true, nil
  1113→	})
  1114→}

  1119→		cachedPods, err := testCtx.Scheduler.Cache.PodCount()
  1120→		if err != nil {

  1122→		}
  1123→		if len(pods) != cachedPods {
  1124→			return false, nil
  1125→		}
  1126→		for _, p := range pods {
  1127→			actualPod, err1 := testCtx.ClientSet.CoreV1().Pods(p.Namespace).Get(testCtx.Ctx, p.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
  1128→			if err1 != nil {
  1129→				return false, err1
  1130→			}
  1131→			cachedPod, err2 := testCtx.Scheduler.Cache.GetPod(actualPod)
  1132→			if err2 != nil || cachedPod == nil {

  1149→func CreateNamespacesWithLabels(cs clientset.Interface, namespaces []string, labels map[string]string) error {
  1150→	for _, n := range namespaces {

  1159→// timeout returns a timeout error if the given `f` function doesn't
  1160→// complete within `d` duration; otherwise it returns nil.
  1161→func timeout(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration, f func()) error {
  1162→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, d)
  1163→	defer cancel()
  1164→
  1165→	done := make(chan struct{})
  1166→	go func() {
  1167→		f()
  1168→		close(done)
  1169→	}()
  1170→
  1171→	select {
  1172→	case <-done:
  1173→		return nil
  1174→	case <-ctx.Done():
  1175→		return ctx.Err()
  1176→	}
  1177→}

  1179→// NextPodOrDie returns the next Pod in the scheduler queue.
  1180→// The operation needs to be completed within 5 seconds; otherwise the test gets aborted.
  1181→func NextPodOrDie(t *testing.T, testCtx *TestContext) *schedulerframework.QueuedPodInfo {
  1182→	t.Helper()
  1183→
  1184→	var podInfo *schedulerframework.QueuedPodInfo
  1185→	logger := klog.FromContext(testCtx.Ctx)
  1186→	// NextPod() is a blocking operation. Wrap it in timeout() to avoid relying on
  1187→	// default go testing timeout (10m) to abort.
  1188→	if err := timeout(testCtx.Ctx, time.Second*5, func() {
  1189→		podInfo, _ = testCtx.Scheduler.NextPod(logger)
  1190→	}); err != nil {
  1191→		t.Fatalf("Timed out waiting for the Pod to be popped: %v", err)
  1192→	}
  1193→	return podInfo
  1194→}

    64→// TestConcurrentEvictionRequests is to make sure pod disruption budgets (PDB) controller is able to
    65→// handle concurrent eviction requests. Original issue:#37605
    66→func TestConcurrentEvictionRequests(t *testing.T) {
    67→	podNameFormat := "test-pod-%d"
    68→
    69→	tCtx := ktesting.Init(t)
    70→	closeFn, rm, informers, _, clientSet := rmSetup(tCtx, t)
    71→	defer closeFn()
    72→
    73→	ns := framework.CreateNamespaceOrDie(clientSet, "concurrent-eviction-requests", t)
    74→	defer framework.DeleteNamespaceOrDie(clientSet, ns, t)
    75→	defer tCtx.Cancel("test has completed")
    76→
    77→	informers.Start(tCtx.Done())
    78→	go rm.Run(tCtx)
    79→
    80→	var gracePeriodSeconds int64 = 30
    81→	deleteOption := metav1.DeleteOptions{
    82→		GracePeriodSeconds: &gracePeriodSeconds,
    83→	}
    84→
    85→	// Generate numOfEvictions pods to evict
    86→	for i := range numOfEvictions {
    87→		podName := fmt.Sprintf(podNameFormat, i)
    88→		pod := newPod(podName)
    89→
    90→		if _, err := clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).Create(context.TODO(), pod, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {
    91→			t.Errorf("Failed to create pod: %v", err)
    92→		}
    93→		pod.Status.Phase = v1.PodRunning
    94→		addPodConditionReady(pod)
    95→		if _, err := clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), pod, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {
    96→			t.Fatal(err)
    97→		}
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	waitToObservePods(t, informers.Core().V1().Pods().Informer(), numOfEvictions, v1.PodRunning)
   101→
   102→	pdb := newPDB()
   103→	if _, err := clientSet.PolicyV1().PodDisruptionBudgets(ns.Name).Create(context.TODO(), pdb, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {
   104→		t.Errorf("Failed to create PodDisruptionBudget: %v", err)
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	waitPDBStable(t, clientSet, ns.Name, pdb.Name, numOfEvictions)
   108→
   109→	var numberPodsEvicted atomic.Uint32
   110→	errCh := make(chan error, 3*numOfEvictions)
   111→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
   112→	// spawn numOfEvictions goroutines to concurrently evict the pods
   113→	for i := range numOfEvictions {
   114→		wg.Add(1)
   115→		go func(id int, errCh chan error) {
   116→			defer wg.Done()
   117→			podName := fmt.Sprintf(podNameFormat, id)
   118→			eviction := newV1Eviction(ns.Name, podName, deleteOption)
   119→
   120→			err := wait.PollImmediate(5*time.Second, 60*time.Second, func() (bool, error) {
   121→				e := clientSet.PolicyV1().Evictions(ns.Name).Evict(context.TODO(), eviction)
   122→				switch {
   123→				case apierrors.IsTooManyRequests(e):
   124→					return false, nil
   125→				case apierrors.IsConflict(e):
   126→					return false, fmt.Errorf("Unexpected Conflict (409) error caused by failing to handle concurrent PDB updates: %v", e)
   127→				case e == nil:
   128→					return true, nil
   129→				default:
   130→					return false, e
   131→				}
   132→			})
   133→
   134→			if err != nil {
   135→				errCh <- err
   136→				// should not return here otherwise we would leak the pod
   137→			}
   138→
   139→			_, err = clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).Get(context.TODO(), podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   140→			switch {
   141→			case apierrors.IsNotFound(err):
   142→				numberPodsEvicted.Add(1)
   143→				// pod was evicted and deleted so return from goroutine immediately
   144→				return
   145→			case err == nil:
   146→				// this shouldn't happen if the pod was evicted successfully
   147→				errCh <- fmt.Errorf("Pod %q is expected to be evicted", podName)
   148→			default:
   149→				errCh <- err
   150→			}

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software

WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
*/

package evictions

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"reflect"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"sync/atomic"
	"testing"
	"time"

	v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
	policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1"
	policyv1beta1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1beta1"
	apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
	utilerrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/uuid"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
	cacheddiscovery "k8s.io/client-go/discovery/cached/memory"
	"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic"
	"k8s.io/client-go/informers"
	clientset "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
	policyv1client "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/policy/v1"
	restclient "k8s.io/client-go/rest"
	"k8s.io/client-go/restmapper"
	"k8s.io/client-go/scale"
	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache"
	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
	kubeapiservertesting "k8s.io/kubernetes/cmd/kube-apiserver/app/testing"
	podutil "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/disruption"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/test/integration/framework"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/test/utils/ktesting"
)

const (
	numOfEvictions = 10
)

// TestConcurrentEvictionRequests is to make sure pod disruption budgets (PDB) controller is able to
// handle concurrent eviction requests. Original issue:#37605
func TestConcurrentEvictionRequests(t *testing.T) {
	podNameFormat := "test-pod-%d"

	tCtx := ktesting.Init(t)
	closeFn, rm, informers, _, clientSet := rmSetup(tCtx, t)
	defer closeFn()

	ns := framework.CreateNamespaceOrDie(clientSet, "concurrent-eviction-requests", t)
	defer framework.DeleteNamespaceOrDie(clientSet, ns, t)
	defer tCtx.Cancel("test has completed")

	informers.Start(tCtx.Done())
	go rm.Run(tCtx)

	var gracePeriodSeconds int64 = 30
	deleteOption := metav1.DeleteOptions{
		GracePeriodSeconds: &gracePeriodSeconds,
	}

	// Generate numOfEvictions pods to evict
	for i := range numOfEvictions {
		podName := fmt.Sprintf(podNameFormat, i)
		pod := newPod(podName)

		if _, err := clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).Create(context.TODO(), pod, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {
			t.Errorf("Failed to create pod: %v", err)
		}
		pod.Status.Phase = v1.PodRunning
		addPodConditionReady(pod)
		if _, err := clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), pod, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {
			t.Fatal(err)
		}
	}

	waitToObservePods(t, informers.Core().V1().Pods().Informer(), numOfEvictions, v1.PodRunning)

	pdb := newPDB()
	if _, err := clientSet.PolicyV1().PodDisruptionBudgets(ns.Name).Create(context.TODO(), pdb, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {
		t.Errorf("Failed to create PodDisruptionBudget: %v", err)
	}

	waitPDBStable(t, clientSet, ns.Name, pdb.Name, numOfEvictions)

	var numberPodsEvicted atomic.Uint32
	errCh := make(chan error, 3*numOfEvictions)
	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	// spawn numOfEvictions goroutines to concurrently evict the pods
	for i := range numOfEvictions {
		wg.Add(1)
		go func(id int, errCh chan error) {
			defer wg.Done()
			podName := fmt.Sprintf(podNameFormat, id)
			eviction := newV1Eviction(ns.Name, podName, deleteOption)

			err := wait.PollImmediate(5*time.Second, 60*time.Second, func() (bool, error) {
				e := clientSet.PolicyV1().Evictions(ns.Name).Evict(context.TODO(), eviction)
				switch {
				case apierrors.IsTooManyRequests(e):
					return false, nil
				case apierrors.IsConflict(e):
					return false, fmt.Errorf("Unexpected Conflict (409) error caused by failing to handle concurrent PDB updates: %v", e)
				case e == nil:
					return true, nil
				default:
					return false, e
				}
			})

			if err != nil {
				errCh <- err
				// should not return here otherwise we would leak the pod
			}

			_, err = clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).Get(context.TODO(), podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
			switch {
			case apierrors.IsNotFound(err):
				numberPodsEvicted.Add(1)
				// pod was evicted and deleted so return from goroutine immediately
				return
			case err == nil:
				// this shouldn't happen if the pod was evicted successfully
				errCh <- fmt.Errorf("Pod %q is expected to be evicted", podName)
			default:
				errCh <- err
			}

     8→    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9→
    10→Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11→distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12→WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.
    15→*/
    16→
    17→package evictions
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"context"
    21→	"encoding/json"
    22→	"fmt"
    23→	"reflect"
    24→	"strings"
    25→	"sync"
    26→	"sync/atomic"
    27→	"testing"
    28→	"time"
    29→
    30→	v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    31→	policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1"
    32→	policyv1beta1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1beta1"
    33→	apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
    34→	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
    35→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured"
    36→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    37→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
    38→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
    39→	utilerrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors"
    40→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr"
    41→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/uuid"
    42→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
    43→	cacheddiscovery "k8s.io/client-go/discovery/cached/memory"
    44→	"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic"
    45→	"k8s.io/client-go/informers"
    46→	clientset "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
    47→	policyv1client "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/policy/v1"
    48→	restclient "k8s.io/client-go/rest"
    49→	"k8s.io/client-go/restmapper"
    50→	"k8s.io/client-go/scale"
    51→	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache"
    52→	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    53→	kubeapiservertesting "k8s.io/kubernetes/cmd/kube-apiserver/app/testing"
    54→	podutil "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod"
    55→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/disruption"
    56→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/test/integration/framework"
    57→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/test/utils/ktesting"
    58→)
    59→
    60→const (
    61→	numOfEvictions = 10
    62→)
    63→
    64→// TestConcurrentEvictionRequests is to make sure pod disruption budgets (PDB) controller is able to
    65→// handle concurrent eviction requests. Original issue:#37605
    66→func TestConcurrentEvictionRequests(t *testing.T) {
    67→	podNameFormat := "test-pod-%d"
    68→
    69→	tCtx := ktesting.Init(t)
    70→	closeFn, rm, informers, _, clientSet := rmSetup(tCtx, t)
    71→	defer closeFn()
    72→
    73→	ns := framework.CreateNamespaceOrDie(clientSet, "concurrent-eviction-requests", t)
    74→	defer framework.DeleteNamespaceOrDie(clientSet, ns, t)
    75→	defer tCtx.Cancel("test has completed")
    76→
    77→	informers.Start(tCtx.Done())
    78→	go rm.Run(tCtx)
    79→
    80→	var gracePeriodSeconds int64 = 30
    81→	deleteOption := metav1.DeleteOptions{
    82→		GracePeriodSeconds: &gracePeriodSeconds,
    83→	}
    84→
    85→	// Generate numOfEvictions pods to evict
    86→	for i := range numOfEvictions {
    87→		podName := fmt.Sprintf(podNameFormat, i)
    88→		pod := newPod(podName)
    89→
    90→		if _, err := clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).Create(context.TODO(), pod, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {
    91→			t.Errorf("Failed to create pod: %v", err)
    92→		}
    93→		pod.Status.Phase = v1.PodRunning
    94→		addPodConditionReady(pod)
    95→		if _, err := clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), pod, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {
    96→			t.Fatal(err)
    97→		}
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	waitToObservePods(t, informers.Core().V1().Pods().Informer(), numOfEvictions, v1.PodRunning)
   101→
   102→	pdb := newPDB()
   103→	if _, err := clientSet.PolicyV1().PodDisruptionBudgets(ns.Name).Create(context.TODO(), pdb, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {
   104→		t.Errorf("Failed to create PodDisruptionBudget: %v", err)
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	waitPDBStable(t, clientSet, ns.Name, pdb.Name, numOfEvictions)
   108→
   109→	var numberPodsEvicted atomic.Uint32
   110→	errCh := make(chan error, 3*numOfEvictions)
   111→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
   112→	// spawn numOfEvictions goroutines to concurrently evict the pods
   113→	for i := range numOfEvictions {
   114→		wg.Add(1)
   115→		go func(id int, errCh chan error) {
   116→			defer wg.Done()
   117→			podName := fmt.Sprintf(podNameFormat, id)
   118→			eviction := newV1Eviction(ns.Name, podName, deleteOption)
   119→
   120→			err := wait.PollImmediate(5*time.Second, 60*time.Second, func() (bool, error) {
   121→				e := clientSet.PolicyV1().Evictions(ns.Name).Evict(context.TODO(), eviction)
   122→				switch {
   123→				case apierrors.IsTooManyRequests(e):
   124→					return false, nil
   125→				case apierrors.IsConflict(e):
   126→					return false, fmt.Errorf("Unexpected Conflict (409) error caused by failing to handle concurrent PDB updates: %v", e)
   127→				case e == nil:
   128→					return true, nil
   129→				default:
   130→					return false, e
   131→				}
   132→			})
   133→
   134→			if err != nil {
   135→				errCh <- err
   136→				// should not return here otherwise we would leak the pod
   137→			}
   138→
   139→			_, err = clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(ns.Name).Get(context.TODO(), podName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   140→			switch {
   141→			case apierrors.IsNotFound(err):
   142→				numberPodsEvicted.Add(1)
   143→				// pod was evicted and deleted so return from goroutine immediately
   144→				return
   145→			case err == nil:
   146→				// this shouldn't happen if the pod was evicted successfully
   147→				errCh <- fmt.Errorf("Pod %q is expected to be evicted", podName)
   148→			default:
   149→				errCh <- err
   150→			}

    59→type pdbStates map[string]policy.PodDisruptionBudget
    60→
    61→var alwaysReady = func() bool { return true }
    62→
    63→func (ps *pdbStates) Set(ctx context.Context, pdb *policy.PodDisruptionBudget) error {
    64→	key, err := controller.KeyFunc(pdb)
    65→	if err != nil {
    66→		return err
    67→	}
    68→	(*ps)[key] = *pdb.DeepCopy()
    69→	return nil
    70→}
    71→
    72→func (ps *pdbStates) Get(key string) policy.PodDisruptionBudget {
    73→	return (*ps)[key]
    74→}
    75→
    76→func (ps *pdbStates) VerifyPdbStatus(t *testing.T, key string, disruptionsAllowed, currentHealthy, desiredHealthy, expectedPods int32, disruptedPodMap map[string]metav1.Time) {
    77→	t.Helper()
    78→	actualPDB := ps.Get(key)
    79→	actualConditions := actualPDB.Status.Conditions
    80→	actualPDB.Status.Conditions = nil
    81→	expectedStatus := policy.PodDisruptionBudgetStatus{
    82→		DisruptionsAllowed: disruptionsAllowed,
    83→		CurrentHealthy:     currentHealthy,
    84→		DesiredHealthy:     desiredHealthy,
    85→		ExpectedPods:       expectedPods,
    86→		DisruptedPods:      disruptedPodMap,
    87→		ObservedGeneration: actualPDB.Generation,
    88→	}
    89→	actualStatus := actualPDB.Status
    90→	if diff := cmp.Diff(expectedStatus, actualStatus, cmpopts.EquateEmpty()); diff != "" {
    91→		t.Fatalf("PDB %q status mismatch (-want,+got):\n%s", key, diff)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	cond := apimeta.FindStatusCondition(actualConditions, policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition)
    95→	if cond == nil {
    96→		t.Fatalf("Expected condition %q, but didn't find it", policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition)
    97→	}
    98→	if disruptionsAllowed > 0 {
    99→		if cond.Status != metav1.ConditionTrue {
   100→			t.Fatalf("Expected condition %q to have status %q, but was %q",
   101→				policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition, metav1.ConditionTrue, cond.Status)
   102→		}
   103→	} else {
   104→		if cond.Status != metav1.ConditionFalse {
   105→			t.Fatalf("Expected condition %q to have status %q, but was %q",
   106→				policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition, metav1.ConditionFalse, cond.Status)
   107→		}
   108→	}
   109→}
   110→
   111→func (ps *pdbStates) VerifyDisruptionAllowed(t *testing.T, key string, disruptionsAllowed int32) {
   112→	pdb := ps.Get(key)
   113→	if pdb.Status.DisruptionsAllowed != disruptionsAllowed {
   114→		debug.PrintStack()
   115→		t.Fatalf("PodDisruptionAllowed mismatch for PDB %q.  Expected %v but got %v.", key, disruptionsAllowed, pdb.Status.DisruptionsAllowed)
   116→	}
   117→}
   118→
   119→func (ps *pdbStates) VerifyNoStatusError(t *testing.T, key string) {
   120→	pdb := ps.Get(key)
   121→	for _, condition := range pdb.Status.Conditions {
   122→		if strings.Contains(condition.Message, "found no controller ref") && condition.Reason == policy.SyncFailedReason {
   123→			t.Fatalf("PodDisruption Controller should not error when unmanaged pods are found but it failed for %q", key)
   124→		}
   125→	}
   126→}

   140→	discoveryClient *discoveryfake.FakeDiscovery
   141→	informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory
   142→}
   143→

   145→	Group:   "custom.k8s.io",
   146→	Version: "v1",
   147→	Kind:    "customresource",
   148→}

    50→	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
    51→	"k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue"

    53→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller"
    54→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/test/utils/ktesting"
    55→	clocktesting "k8s.io/utils/clock/testing"
    56→	"k8s.io/utils/ptr"
    57→)
    58→
    59→type pdbStates map[string]policy.PodDisruptionBudget
    60→
    61→var alwaysReady = func() bool { return true }
    62→
    63→func (ps *pdbStates) Set(ctx context.Context, pdb *policy.PodDisruptionBudget) error {
    64→	key, err := controller.KeyFunc(pdb)
    65→	if err != nil {
    66→		return err
    67→	}
    68→	(*ps)[key] = *pdb.DeepCopy()
    69→	return nil
    70→}
    71→
    72→func (ps *pdbStates) Get(key string) policy.PodDisruptionBudget {
    73→	return (*ps)[key]
    74→}
    75→
    76→func (ps *pdbStates) VerifyPdbStatus(t *testing.T, key string, disruptionsAllowed, currentHealthy, desiredHealthy, expectedPods int32, disruptedPodMap map[string]metav1.Time) {
    77→	t.Helper()
    78→	actualPDB := ps.Get(key)
    79→	actualConditions := actualPDB.Status.Conditions
    80→	actualPDB.Status.Conditions = nil
    81→	expectedStatus := policy.PodDisruptionBudgetStatus{
    82→		DisruptionsAllowed: disruptionsAllowed,
    83→		CurrentHealthy:     currentHealthy,
    84→		DesiredHealthy:     desiredHealthy,
    85→		ExpectedPods:       expectedPods,
    86→		DisruptedPods:      disruptedPodMap,
    87→		ObservedGeneration: actualPDB.Generation,
    88→	}
    89→	actualStatus := actualPDB.Status
    90→	if diff := cmp.Diff(expectedStatus, actualStatus, cmpopts.EquateEmpty()); diff != "" {
    91→		t.Fatalf("PDB %q status mismatch (-want,+got):\n%s", key, diff)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	cond := apimeta.FindStatusCondition(actualConditions, policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition)
    95→	if cond == nil {
    96→		t.Fatalf("Expected condition %q, but didn't find it", policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition)
    97→	}
    98→	if disruptionsAllowed > 0 {
    99→		if cond.Status != metav1.ConditionTrue {
   100→			t.Fatalf("Expected condition %q to have status %q, but was %q",
   101→				policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition, metav1.ConditionTrue, cond.Status)
   102→		}
   103→	} else {
   104→		if cond.Status != metav1.ConditionFalse {
   105→			t.Fatalf("Expected condition %q to have status %q, but was %q",
   106→				policy.DisruptionAllowedCondition, metav1.ConditionFalse, cond.Status)
   107→		}
   108→	}
   109→}
   110→
   111→func (ps *pdbStates) VerifyDisruptionAllowed(t *testing.T, key string, disruptionsAllowed int32) {
   112→	pdb := ps.Get(key)
   113→	if pdb.Status.DisruptionsAllowed != disruptionsAllowed {
   114→		debug.PrintStack()
   115→		t.Fatalf("PodDisruptionAllowed mismatch for PDB %q.  Expected %v but got %v.", key, disruptionsAllowed, pdb.Status.DisruptionsAllowed)
   116→	}
   117→}
   118→
   119→func (ps *pdbStates) VerifyNoStatusError(t *testing.T, key string) {
   120→	pdb := ps.Get(key)
   121→	for _, condition := range pdb.Status.Conditions {
   122→		if strings.Contains(condition.Message, "found no controller ref") && condition.Reason == policy.SyncFailedReason {
   123→			t.Fatalf("PodDisruption Controller should not error when unmanaged pods are found but it failed for %q", key)
   124→		}
   125→	}
   126→}
   127→
   128→type disruptionController struct {
   129→	*DisruptionController
   130→
   131→	podStore cache.Store
   132→	pdbStore cache.Store
   133→	rcStore  cache.Store
   134→	rsStore  cache.Store
   135→	dStore   cache.Store
   136→	ssStore  cache.Store
   137→
   138→	coreClient      *fake.Clientset
   139→	scaleClient     *scalefake.FakeScaleClient
   140→	discoveryClient *discoveryfake.FakeDiscovery
   141→	informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory
   142→}
   143→
   144→var customGVK = schema.GroupVersionKind{
   145→	Group:   "custom.k8s.io",
   146→	Version: "v1",
   147→	Kind:    "customresource",
   148→}
   149→
   150→func newFakeDisruptionController(ctx context.Context) (*disruptionController, *pdbStates) {

    51→	tests := []struct {

    91→

    97→		},
    98→		{
    99→			name:          "proportional scaling: 5 -> 3",
   100→			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 3, nil, nil, nil, nil),
   101→			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 5, nil, nil, nil, nil),
   102→

/*

    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
*/

package deployment

import (
	"math"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	apps "k8s.io/api/apps/v1"
	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation"
	"k8s.io/client-go/informers"
	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/fake"
	testclient "k8s.io/client-go/testing"
	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
	"k8s.io/klog/v2/ktesting"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller"
	deploymentutil "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/deployment/util"
	"k8s.io/utils/ptr"
)

func TestScale(t *testing.T) {
	newTimestamp := metav1.Date(2016, 5, 20, 2, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	oldTimestamp := metav1.Date(2016, 5, 20, 1, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	olderTimestamp := metav1.Date(2016, 5, 20, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)

	var updatedTemplate = func(replicas int32) *apps.Deployment {
		d := newDeployment("foo", replicas, nil, nil, nil, map[string]string{"foo": "bar"})
		d.Spec.Template.Labels["another"] = "label"
		return d
	}

	tests := []struct {
		name          string
		deployment    *apps.Deployment
		oldDeployment *apps.Deployment

		newRS  *apps.ReplicaSet
		oldRSs []*apps.ReplicaSet

		expectedNew  *apps.ReplicaSet
		expectedOld  []*apps.ReplicaSet
		wasntUpdated map[string]bool

		desiredReplicasAnnotations map[string]int32
	}{
		{
			name:          "normal scaling event: 10 -> 12",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 12, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v1", 10, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v1", 12, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},
		},
		{
			name:          "normal scaling event: 10 -> 5",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 5, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v1", 10, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v1", 5, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 5 -> 10",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 5, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v2", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, oldTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v2", 4, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 6, nil, oldTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 5 -> 3",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 3, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 5, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v2", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, oldTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v2", 1, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 2, nil, oldTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 9 -> 4",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 4, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 9, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v2", 8, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 1, nil, oldTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v2", 4, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 0, nil, oldTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 7 -> 10",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 7, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v3", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 3, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 2, nil, olderTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v3", 3, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 4, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, olderTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 13 -> 8",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 8, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 13, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v3", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 8, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, olderTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v3", 1, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 5, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 2, nil, olderTimestamp)},
		},
		// Scales up the new replica set.
		{
			name:          "leftover distribution: 3 -> 4",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 4, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 3, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v3", 1, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 1, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 1, nil, olderTimestamp)},

WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
*/

package deployment

import (
	"math"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	apps "k8s.io/api/apps/v1"
	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation"
	"k8s.io/client-go/informers"
	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/fake"
	testclient "k8s.io/client-go/testing"
	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
	"k8s.io/klog/v2/ktesting"
	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller"
	deploymentutil "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/deployment/util"
	"k8s.io/utils/ptr"
)

func TestScale(t *testing.T) {
	newTimestamp := metav1.Date(2016, 5, 20, 2, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	oldTimestamp := metav1.Date(2016, 5, 20, 1, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	olderTimestamp := metav1.Date(2016, 5, 20, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)

	var updatedTemplate = func(replicas int32) *apps.Deployment {
		d := newDeployment("foo", replicas, nil, nil, nil, map[string]string{"foo": "bar"})
		d.Spec.Template.Labels["another"] = "label"
		return d
	}

	tests := []struct {
		name          string
		deployment    *apps.Deployment
		oldDeployment *apps.Deployment

		newRS  *apps.ReplicaSet
		oldRSs []*apps.ReplicaSet

		expectedNew  *apps.ReplicaSet
		expectedOld  []*apps.ReplicaSet
		wasntUpdated map[string]bool

		desiredReplicasAnnotations map[string]int32
	}{
		{
			name:          "normal scaling event: 10 -> 12",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 12, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v1", 10, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v1", 12, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},
		},
		{
			name:          "normal scaling event: 10 -> 5",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 5, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v1", 10, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v1", 5, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 5 -> 10",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 5, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v2", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, oldTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v2", 4, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 6, nil, oldTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 5 -> 3",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 3, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 5, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v2", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, oldTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v2", 1, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 2, nil, oldTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 9 -> 4",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 4, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 9, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v2", 8, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 1, nil, oldTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v2", 4, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v1", 0, nil, oldTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 7 -> 10",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 10, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 7, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v3", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 3, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 2, nil, olderTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v3", 3, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 4, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, olderTimestamp)},
		},
		{
			name:          "proportional scaling: 13 -> 8",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 8, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 13, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v3", 2, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 8, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 3, nil, olderTimestamp)},

			expectedNew: rs("foo-v3", 1, nil, newTimestamp),
			expectedOld: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 5, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 2, nil, olderTimestamp)},
		},
		// Scales up the new replica set.
		{
			name:          "leftover distribution: 3 -> 4",
			deployment:    newDeployment("foo", 4, nil, nil, nil, nil),
			oldDeployment: newDeployment("foo", 3, nil, nil, nil, nil),

			newRS:  rs("foo-v3", 1, nil, newTimestamp),
			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{rs("foo-v2", 1, nil, oldTimestamp), rs("foo-v1", 1, nil, olderTimestamp)},

    61→			Labels:          template.Labels,

    63→		},
    64→		Spec: apps.ReplicaSetSpec{

    67→			Selector: &metav1.LabelSelector{MatchLabels: template.Labels},
    68→		},
    69→	}

    72→func randomUID() types.UID {

   119→	}

    33→
    34→func TestScaleDownOldReplicaSets(t *testing.T) {
    35→	tests := []struct {
    36→		oldRSSizes []int32
    37→		d          *apps.Deployment
    38→	}{
    39→		{
    40→			oldRSSizes: []int32{3},
    41→			d:          newDeployment("foo", 3, nil, nil, nil, map[string]string{"foo": "bar"}),
    42→		},
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	for i := range tests {
    46→		t.Logf("running scenario %d", i)
    47→		test := tests[i]
    48→
    49→		var oldRSs []*apps.ReplicaSet
    50→		var expected []runtime.Object
    51→
    52→		for n, size := range test.oldRSSizes {
    53→			rs := newReplicaSet(test.d, fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d", test.d.Name, n), size)
    54→			oldRSs = append(oldRSs, rs)
    55→
    56→			rsCopy := rs.DeepCopy()
    57→
    58→			zero := int32(0)
    59→			rsCopy.Spec.Replicas = &zero
    60→			expected = append(expected, rsCopy)
    61→
    62→			if *(oldRSs[n].Spec.Replicas) == *(expected[n].(*apps.ReplicaSet).Spec.Replicas) {
    63→				t.Errorf("broken test - original and expected RS have the same size")
    64→			}
    65→		}
    66→
    67→		kc := fake.NewSimpleClientset(expected...)
    68→		informers := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(kc, controller.NoResyncPeriodFunc())
    69→		_, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)
    70→		c, err := NewDeploymentController(ctx, informers.Apps().V1().Deployments(), informers.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets(), informers.Core().V1().Pods(), kc)
    71→		if err != nil {
    72→			t.Fatalf("error creating Deployment controller: %v", err)
    73→		}
    74→		c.eventRecorder = &record.FakeRecorder{}
    75→
    76→		c.scaleDownOldReplicaSetsForRecreate(ctx, oldRSs, test.d)
    77→		for j := range oldRSs {
    78→			rs := oldRSs[j]
    79→
    80→			if *rs.Spec.Replicas != 0 {
    81→				t.Errorf("rs %q has non-zero replicas", rs.Name)
    82→			}
    83→		}
    84→	}
    85→}
    86→
    87→func TestOldPodsRunning(t *testing.T) {
    88→	tests := []struct {
    89→		name string
    90→
    91→		newRS  *apps.ReplicaSet
    92→		oldRSs []*apps.ReplicaSet
    93→		podMap map[types.UID][]*v1.Pod
    94→
    95→		hasOldPodsRunning bool
    96→	}{
    97→		{
    98→			name:              "no old RSs",
    99→			hasOldPodsRunning: false,
   100→		},
   101→		{
   102→			name:              "old RSs with running pods",
   103→			oldRSs:            []*apps.ReplicaSet{rsWithUID("some-uid"), rsWithUID("other-uid")},
   104→			podMap:            podMapWithUIDs([]string{"some-uid", "other-uid"}),
   105→			hasOldPodsRunning: true,
   106→		},
   107→		{
   108→			name:              "old RSs without pods but with non-zero status replicas",
   109→			oldRSs:            []*apps.ReplicaSet{newRSWithStatus("rs-1", 0, 1, nil)},
   110→			hasOldPodsRunning: true,
   111→		},
   112→		{
   113→			name:              "old RSs without pods or non-zero status replicas",
   114→			oldRSs:            []*apps.ReplicaSet{newRSWithStatus("rs-1", 0, 0, nil)},
   115→			hasOldPodsRunning: false,
   116→		},
   117→		{
   118→			name:   "old RSs with zero status replicas but pods in terminal state are present",
   119→			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{newRSWithStatus("rs-1", 0, 0, nil)},
   120→			podMap: map[types.UID][]*v1.Pod{
   121→				"uid-1": {
   122→					{
   123→						Status: v1.PodStatus{
   124→							Phase: v1.PodFailed,
   125→						},
   126→					},
   127→					{
   128→						Status: v1.PodStatus{
   129→							Phase: v1.PodSucceeded,
   130→						},
   131→					},
   132→				},
   133→			},
   134→			hasOldPodsRunning: false,
   135→		},
   136→		{
   137→			name:   "old RSs with zero status replicas but pod in unknown phase present",
   138→			oldRSs: []*apps.ReplicaSet{newRSWithStatus("rs-1", 0, 0, nil)},
   139→			podMap: map[types.UID][]*v1.Pod{
   140→				"uid-1": {
   141→					{
   142→						Status: v1.PodStatus{
   143→							Phase: v1.PodUnknown,
   144→						},
   145→					},
   146→				},
   147→			},
   148→			hasOldPodsRunning: true,
   149→		},
   150→		{

1→/*
     2→Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.
     3→
     4→Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5→you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6→You may obtain a copy of the License at

    11→distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12→WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.
    15→*/
    16→
    17→package target
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"context"
    21→	"errors"
    22→	"fmt"
    23→	"time"
    24→
    25→	appsv1 "k8s.io/api/apps/v1"
    26→	batchv1 "k8s.io/api/batch/v1"
    27→	corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    28→	apimeta "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"
    29→	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
    30→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels"
    31→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
    32→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
    33→	"k8s.io/client-go/discovery"
    34→	cacheddiscovery "k8s.io/client-go/discovery/cached/memory"
    35→	"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic"
    36→	"k8s.io/client-go/informers"
    37→	kube_client "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
    38→	"k8s.io/client-go/rest"
    39→	"k8s.io/client-go/restmapper"
    40→	"k8s.io/client-go/scale"
    41→	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache"
    42→	klog "k8s.io/klog/v2"
    43→
    44→	vpa_types "k8s.io/autoscaler/vertical-pod-autoscaler/pkg/apis/autoscaling.k8s.io/v1"
    45→)
    46→
    47→const (
    48→	discoveryResetPeriod time.Duration = 5 * time.Minute
    49→)
    50→
    51→// VpaTargetSelectorFetcher gets a labelSelector used to gather Pods controlled by the given VPA.
    52→type VpaTargetSelectorFetcher interface {
    53→	// Fetch returns a labelSelector used to gather Pods controlled by the given VPA.
    54→	// If error is nil, the returned labelSelector is not nil.
    55→	Fetch(ctx context.Context, vpa *vpa_types.VerticalPodAutoscaler) (labels.Selector, error)
    56→}
    57→
    58→type wellKnownController string
    59→
    60→const (
    61→	daemonSet             wellKnownController = "DaemonSet"
    62→	deployment            wellKnownController = "Deployment"
    63→	replicaSet            wellKnownController = "ReplicaSet"
    64→	statefulSet           wellKnownController = "StatefulSet"
    65→	replicationController wellKnownController = "ReplicationController"
    66→	job                   wellKnownController = "Job"
    67→	cronJob               wellKnownController = "CronJob"
    68→)
    69→
    70→// NewVpaTargetSelectorFetcher returns new instance of VpaTargetSelectorFetcher
    71→func NewVpaTargetSelectorFetcher(config *rest.Config, kubeClient kube_client.Interface, factory informers.SharedInformerFactory) VpaTargetSelectorFetcher {
    72→	discoveryClient, err := discovery.NewDiscoveryClientForConfig(config)
    73→	if err != nil {
    74→		klog.ErrorS(err, "Could not create discoveryClient")
    75→		klog.FlushAndExit(klog.ExitFlushTimeout, 1)
    76→	}
    77→	resolver := scale.NewDiscoveryScaleKindResolver(discoveryClient)
    78→	restClient := kubeClient.CoreV1().RESTClient()
    79→	cachedDiscoveryClient := cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(discoveryClient)
    80→	mapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(cachedDiscoveryClient)
    81→	go wait.Until(func() {
    82→		mapper.Reset()
    83→	}, discoveryResetPeriod, make(chan struct{}))
    84→
    85→	informersMap := map[wellKnownController]cache.SharedIndexInformer{
    86→		daemonSet:             factory.Apps().V1().DaemonSets().Informer(),
    87→		deployment:            factory.Apps().V1().Deployments().Informer(),
    88→		replicaSet:            factory.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets().Informer(),
    89→		statefulSet:           factory.Apps().V1().StatefulSets().Informer(),
    90→		replicationController: factory.Core().V1().ReplicationControllers().Informer(),
    91→		job:                   factory.Batch().V1().Jobs().Informer(),
    92→		cronJob:               factory.Batch().V1().CronJobs().Informer(),
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	scaleNamespacer := scale.New(restClient, mapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, resolver)
    96→	return &vpaTargetSelectorFetcher{
    97→		scaleNamespacer: scaleNamespacer,
    98→		mapper:          mapper,
    99→		informersMap:    informersMap,
   100→	}
   101→}
   102→
   103→// vpaTargetSelectorFetcher implements VpaTargetSelectorFetcher interface
   104→// by querying API server for the controller pointed by VPA's targetRef
   105→type vpaTargetSelectorFetcher struct {
   106→	scaleNamespacer scale.ScalesGetter
   107→	mapper          apimeta.RESTMapper
   108→	informersMap    map[wellKnownController]cache.SharedIndexInformer
   109→}
   110→
   111→func (f *vpaTargetSelectorFetcher) Fetch(ctx context.Context, vpa *vpa_types.VerticalPodAutoscaler) (labels.Selector, error) {
   112→	if vpa.Spec.TargetRef == nil {
   113→		return nil, errors.New("targetRef not defined. If this is a v1beta1 object, switch to v1")
   114→	}
   115→	kind := wellKnownController(vpa.Spec.TargetRef.Kind)
   116→	informer, exists := f.informersMap[kind]
   117→	if exists {
   118→		return getLabelSelector(informer, vpa.Spec.TargetRef.Kind, vpa.Namespace, vpa.Spec.TargetRef.Name)
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	// not on a list of known controllers, use scale sub-resource
   122→	// TODO: cache response
   123→	groupVersion, err := schema.ParseGroupVersion(vpa.Spec.TargetRef.APIVersion)
   124→	if err != nil {
   125→		return nil, err
   126→	}
   127→	groupKind := schema.GroupKind{
   128→		Group: groupVersion.Group,
   129→		Kind:  vpa.Spec.TargetRef.Kind,
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	selector, err := f.getLabelSelectorFromResource(ctx, groupKind, vpa.Namespace, vpa.Spec.TargetRef.Name)
   133→	if err != nil {
   134→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unhandled targetRef %s / %s / %s, last error %v",
   135→			vpa.Spec.TargetRef.APIVersion, vpa.Spec.TargetRef.Kind, vpa.Spec.TargetRef.Name, err)
   136→	}
   137→	return selector, nil
   138→}
   139→
   140→func getLabelSelector(informer cache.SharedIndexInformer, kind, namespace, name string) (labels.Selector, error) {
   141→	obj, exists, err := informer.GetStore().GetByKey(namespace + "/" + name)
   142→	if err != nil {
   143→		return nil, err
   144→	}
   145→	if !exists {
   146→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s %s/%s does not exist", kind, namespace, name)
   147→	}
   148→	switch apiObj := obj.(type) {
   149→	case (*appsv1.DaemonSet):
   150→		return metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(apiObj.Spec.Selector)
   151→	case (*appsv1.Deployment):
   152→		return metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(apiObj.Spec.Selector)
   153→	case (*appsv1.StatefulSet):
   154→		return metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(apiObj.Spec.Selector)
   155→	case (*appsv1.ReplicaSet):
   156→		return metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(apiObj.Spec.Selector)
   157→	case (*batchv1.Job):
   158→		return metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(apiObj.Spec.Selector)
   159→	case (*batchv1.CronJob):
   160→		return metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(metav1.SetAsLabelSelector(apiObj.Spec.JobTemplate.Spec.Template.Labels))
   161→	case (*corev1.ReplicationController):
   162→		return metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(metav1.SetAsLabelSelector(apiObj.Spec.Selector))
   163→	}
   164→	return nil, errors.New("don't know how to read label selector")
   165→}
   166→
   167→func (f *vpaTargetSelectorFetcher) getLabelSelectorFromResource(
   168→	ctx context.Context, groupKind schema.GroupKind, namespace, name string,
   169→) (labels.Selector, error) {
   170→	mappings, err := f.mapper.RESTMappings(groupKind)
   171→	if err != nil {
   172→		return nil, err
   173→	}
   174→
   175→	var lastError error
   176→	for _, mapping := range mappings {
   177→		groupResource := mapping.Resource.GroupResource()
   178→		scale, err := f.scaleNamespacer.Scales(namespace).Get(ctx, groupResource, name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   179→		if err == nil {
   180→			if scale.Status.Selector == "" {
   181→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resource %s/%s has an empty selector for scale sub-resource", namespace, name)
   182→			}
   183→			selector, err := labels.Parse(scale.Status.Selector)
   184→			if err != nil {
   185→				return nil, err
   186→			}
   187→			return selector, nil
   188→		}
   189→		lastError = err
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	// nothing found, apparently the resource does support scale (or we lack RBAC)
   193→	return nil, lastError
   194→}
   195→

   168→	ctx context.Context, groupKind schema.GroupKind, namespace, name string,
   169→) (labels.Selector, error) {
   170→	mappings, err := f.mapper.RESTMappings(groupKind)
   171→	if err != nil {
   172→		return nil, err
   173→	}
   174→
   175→	var lastError error
   176→	for _, mapping := range mappings {
   177→		groupResource := mapping.Resource.GroupResource()
   178→		scale, err := f.scaleNamespacer.Scales(namespace).Get(ctx, groupResource, name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   179→		if err == nil {
   180→			if scale.Status.Selector == "" {
   181→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resource %s/%s has an empty selector for scale sub-resource", namespace, name)
   182→			}
   183→			selector, err := labels.Parse(scale.Status.Selector)
   184→			if err != nil {
   185→				return nil, err
   186→			}
   187→			return selector, nil
   188→		}
   189→		lastError = err
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	// nothing found, apparently the resource does support scale (or we lack RBAC)
   193→	return nil, lastError
   194→}
   195→

    47→
    48→const (
    49→	cronJob               wellKnownController = "CronJob"
    50→	daemonSet             wellKnownController = "DaemonSet"
    51→	deployment            wellKnownController = "Deployment"
    52→	node                  wellKnownController = "Node"
    53→	job                   wellKnownController = "Job"
    54→	replicaSet            wellKnownController = "ReplicaSet"
    55→	replicationController wellKnownController = "ReplicationController"

    58→
    59→const (
    60→	discoveryResetPeriod time.Duration = 5 * time.Minute

    62→
    63→// ErrNodeInvalidOwner is thrown when a Pod is owned by a Node.
    64→var ErrNodeInvalidOwner = errors.New("node is not a valid owner")
    65→
    66→// ControllerKey identifies a controller.
    67→type ControllerKey struct {
    68→	Namespace string
    69→	Kind      string
    70→	Name      string
    71→}
    72→
    73→// ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion identifies a controller and API it's defined in.
    74→type ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion struct {
    75→	ControllerKey
    76→	ApiVersion string
    77→}
    78→
    79→// ControllerFetcher is responsible for finding the topmost well-known or scalable controller
    80→type ControllerFetcher interface {
    81→	// FindTopMostWellKnownOrScalable returns topmost well-known or scalable controller. Error is returned if controller cannot be found.
    82→	FindTopMostWellKnownOrScalable(ctx context.Context, controller *ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion) (*ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion, error)
    83→}
    84→
    85→type controllerFetcher struct {
    86→	scaleNamespacer              scale.ScalesGetter
    87→	mapper                       apimeta.RESTMapper
    88→	informersMap                 map[wellKnownController]cache.SharedIndexInformer
    89→	scaleSubresourceCacheStorage controllerCacheStorage
    90→}
    91→
    92→func (f *controllerFetcher) periodicallyRefreshCache(ctx context.Context, period time.Duration) {
    93→	for {
    94→		select {
    95→		case <-ctx.Done():
    96→			return
    97→		case <-time.After(period):
    98→			keysToRefresh := f.scaleSubresourceCacheStorage.GetKeysToRefresh()
    99→			klog.V(5).InfoS("Starting to refresh entries in controllerFetchers scaleSubresourceCacheStorage")
   100→			for _, item := range keysToRefresh {
   101→				scale, err := f.scaleNamespacer.Scales(item.namespace).Get(context.TODO(), item.groupResource, item.name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   102→				f.scaleSubresourceCacheStorage.Refresh(item.namespace, item.groupResource, item.name, scale, err)
   103→			}
   104→			klog.V(5).InfoS("Finished refreshing entries in controllerFetchers scaleSubresourceCacheStorage", "refreshed", len(keysToRefresh))
   105→			f.scaleSubresourceCacheStorage.RemoveExpired()
   106→		}
   107→	}
   108→}
   109→
   110→func (f *controllerFetcher) Start(ctx context.Context, loopPeriod time.Duration) {
   111→	go f.periodicallyRefreshCache(ctx, loopPeriod)
   112→}
   113→
   114→// NewControllerFetcher returns a new instance of controllerFetcher
   115→func NewControllerFetcher(config *rest.Config, kubeClient kube_client.Interface, factory informers.SharedInformerFactory, betweenRefreshes, lifeTime time.Duration, jitterFactor float64) *controllerFetcher {
   116→	discoveryClient, err := discovery.NewDiscoveryClientForConfig(config)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		klog.ErrorS(err, "Could not create discoveryClient")
   119→		klog.FlushAndExit(klog.ExitFlushTimeout, 1)
   120→	}
   121→	resolver := scale.NewDiscoveryScaleKindResolver(discoveryClient)
   122→	restClient := kubeClient.CoreV1().RESTClient()
   123→	cachedDiscoveryClient := cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(discoveryClient)
   124→	mapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(cachedDiscoveryClient)
   125→	go wait.Until(func() {
   126→		mapper.Reset()
   127→	}, discoveryResetPeriod, make(chan struct{}))
   128→
   129→	informersMap := map[wellKnownController]cache.SharedIndexInformer{
   130→		daemonSet:             factory.Apps().V1().DaemonSets().Informer(),
   131→		deployment:            factory.Apps().V1().Deployments().Informer(),
   132→		replicaSet:            factory.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets().Informer(),
   133→		statefulSet:           factory.Apps().V1().StatefulSets().Informer(),
   134→		replicationController: factory.Core().V1().ReplicationControllers().Informer(),
   135→		job:                   factory.Batch().V1().Jobs().Informer(),
   136→		cronJob:               factory.Batch().V1().CronJobs().Informer(),
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	scaleNamespacer := scale.New(restClient, mapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, resolver)
   140→	return &controllerFetcher{
   141→		scaleNamespacer:              scaleNamespacer,
   142→		mapper:                       mapper,
   143→		informersMap:                 informersMap,
   144→		scaleSubresourceCacheStorage: newControllerCacheStorage(betweenRefreshes, lifeTime, jitterFactor),
   145→	}
   146→}
   147→
   148→func getOwnerController(owners []metav1.OwnerReference, namespace string) *ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion {
   149→	for _, owner := range owners {
   150→		if owner.Controller != nil && *owner.Controller {
   151→			return &ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion{
   152→				ControllerKey: ControllerKey{
   153→					Namespace: namespace,
   154→					Kind:      owner.Kind,
   155→					Name:      owner.Name,
   156→				},
   157→				ApiVersion: owner.APIVersion,
   158→			}
   159→		}
   160→	}
   161→	return nil
   162→}
   163→
   164→func getParentOfWellKnownController(informer cache.SharedIndexInformer, controllerKey ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion) (*ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion, error) {
   165→	namespace := controllerKey.Namespace
   166→	name := controllerKey.Name
   167→	kind := controllerKey.Kind
   168→
   169→	obj, exists, err := informer.GetStore().GetByKey(namespace + "/" + name)
   170→	if err != nil {
   171→		return nil, err
   172→	}
   173→	if !exists {
   174→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s %s/%s does not exist", kind, namespace, name)
   175→	}
   176→	switch apiObj := obj.(type) {
   177→	case (*appsv1.DaemonSet):
   178→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   179→	case (*appsv1.Deployment):
   180→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   181→	case (*appsv1.StatefulSet):
   182→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   183→	case (*appsv1.ReplicaSet):
   184→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   185→	case (*batchv1.Job):
   186→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   187→	case (*batchv1.CronJob):
   188→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   189→	case (*corev1.ReplicationController):
   190→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   191→	}
   192→
   193→	return nil, errors.New("don't know how to read owner controller")
   194→}
   195→
   196→func (f *controllerFetcher) getParentOfController(ctx context.Context, controllerKey ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion) (*ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion, error) {
   197→	kind := wellKnownController(controllerKey.Kind)
   198→	informer, exists := f.informersMap[kind]
   199→	if exists {
   200→		return getParentOfWellKnownController(informer, controllerKey)

   104→			klog.V(5).InfoS("Finished refreshing entries in controllerFetchers scaleSubresourceCacheStorage", "refreshed", len(keysToRefresh))
   105→			f.scaleSubresourceCacheStorage.RemoveExpired()
   106→		}
   107→	}
   108→}
   109→
   110→func (f *controllerFetcher) Start(ctx context.Context, loopPeriod time.Duration) {
   111→	go f.periodicallyRefreshCache(ctx, loopPeriod)
   112→}
   113→
   114→// NewControllerFetcher returns a new instance of controllerFetcher
   115→func NewControllerFetcher(config *rest.Config, kubeClient kube_client.Interface, factory informers.SharedInformerFactory, betweenRefreshes, lifeTime time.Duration, jitterFactor float64) *controllerFetcher {
   116→	discoveryClient, err := discovery.NewDiscoveryClientForConfig(config)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		klog.ErrorS(err, "Could not create discoveryClient")
   119→		klog.FlushAndExit(klog.ExitFlushTimeout, 1)
   120→	}
   121→	resolver := scale.NewDiscoveryScaleKindResolver(discoveryClient)
   122→	restClient := kubeClient.CoreV1().RESTClient()
   123→	cachedDiscoveryClient := cacheddiscovery.NewMemCacheClient(discoveryClient)
   124→	mapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(cachedDiscoveryClient)
   125→	go wait.Until(func() {
   126→		mapper.Reset()
   127→	}, discoveryResetPeriod, make(chan struct{}))
   128→
   129→	informersMap := map[wellKnownController]cache.SharedIndexInformer{
   130→		daemonSet:             factory.Apps().V1().DaemonSets().Informer(),
   131→		deployment:            factory.Apps().V1().Deployments().Informer(),
   132→		replicaSet:            factory.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets().Informer(),
   133→		statefulSet:           factory.Apps().V1().StatefulSets().Informer(),
   134→		replicationController: factory.Core().V1().ReplicationControllers().Informer(),
   135→		job:                   factory.Batch().V1().Jobs().Informer(),
   136→		cronJob:               factory.Batch().V1().CronJobs().Informer(),
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	scaleNamespacer := scale.New(restClient, mapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, resolver)
   140→	return &controllerFetcher{
   141→		scaleNamespacer:              scaleNamespacer,
   142→		mapper:                       mapper,
   143→		informersMap:                 informersMap,
   144→		scaleSubresourceCacheStorage: newControllerCacheStorage(betweenRefreshes, lifeTime, jitterFactor),
   145→	}
   146→}
   147→
   148→func getOwnerController(owners []metav1.OwnerReference, namespace string) *ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion {
   149→	for _, owner := range owners {
   150→		if owner.Controller != nil && *owner.Controller {
   151→			return &ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion{
   152→				ControllerKey: ControllerKey{
   153→					Namespace: namespace,
   154→					Kind:      owner.Kind,
   155→					Name:      owner.Name,
   156→				},
   157→				ApiVersion: owner.APIVersion,
   158→			}
   159→		}
   160→	}
   161→	return nil
   162→}
   163→
   164→func getParentOfWellKnownController(informer cache.SharedIndexInformer, controllerKey ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion) (*ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion, error) {
   165→	namespace := controllerKey.Namespace
   166→	name := controllerKey.Name
   167→	kind := controllerKey.Kind
   168→
   169→	obj, exists, err := informer.GetStore().GetByKey(namespace + "/" + name)
   170→	if err != nil {
   171→		return nil, err
   172→	}
   173→	if !exists {
   174→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s %s/%s does not exist", kind, namespace, name)
   175→	}
   176→	switch apiObj := obj.(type) {
   177→	case (*appsv1.DaemonSet):
   178→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   179→	case (*appsv1.Deployment):
   180→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   181→	case (*appsv1.StatefulSet):
   182→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   183→	case (*appsv1.ReplicaSet):
   184→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   185→	case (*batchv1.Job):
   186→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   187→	case (*batchv1.CronJob):
   188→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   189→	case (*corev1.ReplicationController):
   190→		return getOwnerController(apiObj.OwnerReferences, namespace), nil
   191→	}
   192→
   193→	return nil, errors.New("don't know how to read owner controller")
   194→}

    62→
    63→// ErrNodeInvalidOwner is thrown when a Pod is owned by a Node.
    64→var ErrNodeInvalidOwner = errors.New("node is not a valid owner")
    65→
    66→// ControllerKey identifies a controller.
    67→type ControllerKey struct {
    68→	Namespace string
    69→	Kind      string
    70→	Name      string

    72→
    73→// ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion identifies a controller and API it's defined in.

    75→	ControllerKey
    76→	ApiVersion string

    79→// ControllerFetcher is responsible for finding the topmost well-known or scalable controller
    80→type ControllerFetcher interface {
    81→	// FindTopMostWellKnownOrScalable returns topmost well-known or scalable controller. Error is returned if controller cannot be found.
    82→	FindTopMostWellKnownOrScalable(ctx context.Context, controller *ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion) (*ControllerKeyWithAPIVersion, error)
    83→}
    84→
    85→type controllerFetcher struct {
    86→	scaleNamespacer              scale.ScalesGetter
    87→	mapper                       apimeta.RESTMapper
    88→	informersMap                 map[wellKnownController]cache.SharedIndexInformer
    89→	scaleSubresourceCacheStorage controllerCacheStorage
    90→}
    91→
    92→func (f *controllerFetcher) periodicallyRefreshCache(ctx context.Context, period time.Duration) {
    93→	for {
    94→		select {
    95→		case <-ctx.Done():
    96→			return
    97→		case <-time.After(period):
    98→			keysToRefresh := f.scaleSubresourceCacheStorage.GetKeysToRefresh()
    99→			klog.V(5).InfoS("Starting to refresh entries in controllerFetchers scaleSubresourceCacheStorage")
   100→			for _, item := range keysToRefresh {
   101→				scale, err := f.scaleNamespacer.Scales(item.namespace).Get(context.TODO(), item.groupResource, item.name, metav1.GetOptions{})
   102→				f.scaleSubresourceCacheStorage.Refresh(item.namespace, item.groupResource, item.name, scale, err)
   103→			}
   104→			klog.V(5).InfoS("Finished refreshing entries in controllerFetchers scaleSubresourceCacheStorage", "refreshed", len(keysToRefresh))
   105→			f.scaleSubresourceCacheStorage.RemoveExpired()
   106→		}
   107→	}
   108→}
   109→
   110→func (f *controllerFetcher) Start(ctx context.Context, loopPeriod time.Duration) {
   111→	go f.periodicallyRefreshCache(ctx, loopPeriod)
   112→}

   114→// NewControllerFetcher returns a new instance of controllerFetcher
   115→func NewControllerFetcher(config *rest.Config, kubeClient kube_client.Interface, factory informers.SharedInformerFactory, betweenRefreshes, lifeTime time.Duration, jitterFactor float64) *controllerFetcher {
   116→	discoveryClient, err := discovery.NewDiscoveryClientForConfig(config)
   117→	if err != nil {

   120→	}

   125→	go wait.Until(func() {

   127→	}, discoveryResetPeriod, make(chan struct{}))
   128→
   129→	informersMap := map[wellKnownController]cache.SharedIndexInformer{
   130→		daemonSet:             factory.Apps().V1().DaemonSets().Informer(),
   131→		deployment:            factory.Apps().V1().Deployments().Informer(),
   132→		replicaSet:            factory.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets().Informer(),
   133→		statefulSet:           factory.Apps().V1().StatefulSets().Informer(),
   134→		replicationController: factory.Core().V1().ReplicationControllers().Informer(),
   135→		job:                   factory.Batch().V1().Jobs().Informer(),
   136→		cronJob:               factory.Batch().V1().CronJobs().Informer(),
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	scaleNamespacer := scale.New(restClient, mapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, resolver)
   140→	return &controllerFetcher{
   141→		scaleNamespacer:              scaleNamespacer,
   142→		mapper:                       mapper,
   143→		informersMap:                 informersMap,
   144→		scaleSubresourceCacheStorage: newControllerCacheStorage(betweenRefreshes, lifeTime, jitterFactor),
   145→	}
   146→}

    54→
    55→// PodTemplateRefIndex is the name of the index for buffers referencing a pod template
    56→const PodTemplateRefIndex = "podTemplateRef"
    57→
    58→// CapacityBufferClient represents client for v1 capacitybuffer CRD.
    59→type CapacityBufferClient struct {
    60→	buffersClient         capacitybuffer.Interface
    61→	kubernetesClient      kubernetes.Interface
    62→	scaleGetter           scaleclient.ScalesGetter
    63→	scaleMapper           meta.RESTMapper
    64→	buffersLister         bufferslisters.CapacityBufferLister
    65→	podTemplateLister     corev1listers.PodTemplateLister
    66→	replicaSetsLister     appsv1listers.ReplicaSetLister
    67→	statefulSetsLister    appsv1listers.StatefulSetLister
    68→	jobsLister            batchv1lister.JobLister
    69→	deploymentLister      appsv1listers.DeploymentLister
    70→	replicationContLister corev1listers.ReplicationControllerLister
    71→	rqLister              corev1listers.ResourceQuotaLister
    72→
    73→	// Informers
    74→	bufferInformer        cache.SharedIndexInformer
    75→	podTemplateInformer   cache.SharedIndexInformer
    76→	resourceQuotaInformer cache.SharedIndexInformer
    77→}
    78→
    79→// NewCapacityBufferClient returns a capacityBufferClient.
    80→func NewCapacityBufferClient(buffersClient capacitybuffer.Interface, kubernetesClient kubernetes.Interface, buffersLister bufferslisters.CapacityBufferLister,
    81→	podTemplateLister corev1listers.PodTemplateLister, replicaSetsLister appsv1listers.ReplicaSetLister, statefulSetsLister appsv1listers.StatefulSetLister,
    82→	jobsLister batchv1lister.JobLister, deploymentLister appsv1listers.DeploymentLister, replicationContLister corev1listers.ReplicationControllerLister,
    83→	scaleGetter scaleclient.ScalesGetter, scaleMapper meta.RESTMapper) (*CapacityBufferClient, error) {
    84→	return &CapacityBufferClient{
    85→		buffersClient:         buffersClient,
    86→		kubernetesClient:      kubernetesClient,
    87→		scaleGetter:           scaleGetter,
    88→		buffersLister:         buffersLister,
    89→		podTemplateLister:     podTemplateLister,
    90→		replicaSetsLister:     replicaSetsLister,
    91→		statefulSetsLister:    statefulSetsLister,
    92→		jobsLister:            jobsLister,
    93→		deploymentLister:      deploymentLister,
    94→		replicationContLister: replicationContLister,
    95→		scaleMapper:           scaleMapper,
    96→	}, nil
    97→}
    98→
    99→// NewCapacityBufferClientFromConfig configures and returns a CapacityBufferClient.
   100→func NewCapacityBufferClientFromConfig(kubeConfig *rest.Config) (*CapacityBufferClient, error) {
   101→	buffersClient, err := capacitybuffer.NewForConfig(kubeConfig)
   102→	if err != nil {
   103→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Failed to create capacity buffer client for capacity buffer: %v", err)
   104→	}
   105→	kubernetesClient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(kubeConfig)
   106→	if err != nil {
   107→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Failed to create kubernetes client for capacity buffer: %v", err)
   108→	}
   109→	scaleGetter, scaleMapper, err := createScaleSubresourceClientGetter(kubeConfig)
   110→	if err != nil {
   111→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Failed to create scale getter for capacity buffer: %v", err)
   112→	}
   113→	return NewCapacityBufferClientFromClients(buffersClient, kubernetesClient, scaleGetter, scaleMapper)
   114→}
   115→
   116→func createScaleSubresourceClientGetter(kubeConfig *rest.Config) (scaleclient.ScalesGetter, meta.RESTMapper, error) {
   117→	discoveryClient, err := discovery.NewDiscoveryClientForConfig(kubeConfig)
   118→	if err != nil {
   119→		return nil, nil, err
   120→	}
   121→	scaleMapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(memory.NewMemCacheClient(discoveryClient))
   122→	scaleKindResolver := scaleclient.NewDiscoveryScaleKindResolver(discoveryClient)
   123→	client, err := scaleclient.NewForConfig(kubeConfig, scaleMapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, scaleKindResolver)
   124→	if err != nil {
   125→		return nil, nil, err
   126→	}
   127→	return client, scaleMapper, nil
   128→}
   129→
   130→// NewCapacityBufferClientFromClients returns a CapacityBufferClient based on the passed clients
   131→func NewCapacityBufferClientFromClients(buffersClient capacitybuffer.Interface, kubernetesClient kubernetes.Interface, scaleGetter scaleclient.ScalesGetter, scaleMapper meta.RESTMapper) (*CapacityBufferClient, error) {
   132→	if buffersClient == nil || kubernetesClient == nil {
   133→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Couldn't create capacity buffer client")
   134→	}
   135→	defaultResyncPeriod := 5 * time.Minute
   136→
   137→	stopChannel := make(chan struct{})
   138→
   139→	buffersFactory := externalversions.NewSharedInformerFactory(buffersClient, defaultResyncPeriod)
   140→	bufferInformer := buffersFactory.Autoscaling().V1beta1().CapacityBuffers().Informer()
   141→	buffersLister := buffersFactory.Autoscaling().V1beta1().CapacityBuffers().Lister()
   142→
   143→	// Add indexer for PodTemplateRef
   144→	err := bufferInformer.AddIndexers(cache.Indexers{
   145→		PodTemplateRefIndex: func(obj interface{}) ([]string, error) {
   146→			buffer, ok := obj.(*v1.CapacityBuffer)
   147→			if !ok {
   148→				return []string{}, nil
   149→			}
   150→			if buffer.Spec.PodTemplateRef != nil {
   151→				return []string{buffer.Spec.PodTemplateRef.Name}, nil
   152→			}
   153→			return []string{}, nil
   154→		},
   155→	})
   156→	if err != nil {
   157→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to add indexers: %v", err)
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	buffersFactory.Start(stopChannel)
   161→	informersSynced := buffersFactory.WaitForCacheSync(stopChannel)
   162→	for _, synced := range informersSynced {
   163→		if !synced {
   164→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("can't create buffers lister")
   165→		}
   166→	}
   167→	klog.V(2).Info("Successful initial buffers sync")
   168→
   169→	factory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(kubernetesClient, defaultResyncPeriod)
   170→	bufferClient := &CapacityBufferClient{
   171→		buffersClient:         buffersClient,
   172→		kubernetesClient:      kubernetesClient,
   173→		scaleGetter:           scaleGetter,
   174→		scaleMapper:           scaleMapper,
   175→		buffersLister:         buffersLister,
   176→		podTemplateLister:     factory.Core().V1().PodTemplates().Lister(),
   177→		replicaSetsLister:     factory.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets().Lister(),
   178→		statefulSetsLister:    factory.Apps().V1().StatefulSets().Lister(),
   179→		jobsLister:            factory.Batch().V1().Jobs().Lister(),

    58→// CapacityBufferClient represents client for v1 capacitybuffer CRD.
    59→type CapacityBufferClient struct {
    60→	buffersClient         capacitybuffer.Interface
    61→	kubernetesClient      kubernetes.Interface
    62→	scaleGetter           scaleclient.ScalesGetter
    63→	scaleMapper           meta.RESTMapper
    64→	buffersLister         bufferslisters.CapacityBufferLister
    65→	podTemplateLister     corev1listers.PodTemplateLister
    66→	replicaSetsLister     appsv1listers.ReplicaSetLister
    67→	statefulSetsLister    appsv1listers.StatefulSetLister
    68→	jobsLister            batchv1lister.JobLister
    69→	deploymentLister      appsv1listers.DeploymentLister
    70→	replicationContLister corev1listers.ReplicationControllerLister
    71→	rqLister              corev1listers.ResourceQuotaLister
    72→
    73→	// Informers
    74→	bufferInformer        cache.SharedIndexInformer
    75→	podTemplateInformer   cache.SharedIndexInformer
    76→	resourceQuotaInformer cache.SharedIndexInformer
    77→}
    78→
    79→// NewCapacityBufferClient returns a capacityBufferClient.
    80→func NewCapacityBufferClient(buffersClient capacitybuffer.Interface, kubernetesClient kubernetes.Interface, buffersLister bufferslisters.CapacityBufferLister,
    81→	podTemplateLister corev1listers.PodTemplateLister, replicaSetsLister appsv1listers.ReplicaSetLister, statefulSetsLister appsv1listers.StatefulSetLister,
    82→	jobsLister batchv1lister.JobLister, deploymentLister appsv1listers.DeploymentLister, replicationContLister corev1listers.ReplicationControllerLister,
    83→	scaleGetter scaleclient.ScalesGetter, scaleMapper meta.RESTMapper) (*CapacityBufferClient, error) {
    84→	return &CapacityBufferClient{
    85→		buffersClient:         buffersClient,
    86→		kubernetesClient:      kubernetesClient,
    87→		scaleGetter:           scaleGetter,
    88→		buffersLister:         buffersLister,
    89→		podTemplateLister:     podTemplateLister,
    90→		replicaSetsLister:     replicaSetsLister,
    91→		statefulSetsLister:    statefulSetsLister,
    92→		jobsLister:            jobsLister,
    93→		deploymentLister:      deploymentLister,
    94→		replicationContLister: replicationContLister,
    95→		scaleMapper:           scaleMapper,
    96→	}, nil
    97→}
    98→
    99→// NewCapacityBufferClientFromConfig configures and returns a CapacityBufferClient.
   100→func NewCapacityBufferClientFromConfig(kubeConfig *rest.Config) (*CapacityBufferClient, error) {
   101→	buffersClient, err := capacitybuffer.NewForConfig(kubeConfig)
   102→	if err != nil {
   103→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Failed to create capacity buffer client for capacity buffer: %v", err)
   104→	}
   105→	kubernetesClient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(kubeConfig)
   106→	if err != nil {
   107→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Failed to create kubernetes client for capacity buffer: %v", err)
   108→	}
   109→	scaleGetter, scaleMapper, err := createScaleSubresourceClientGetter(kubeConfig)
   110→	if err != nil {
   111→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Failed to create scale getter for capacity buffer: %v", err)
   112→	}
   113→	return NewCapacityBufferClientFromClients(buffersClient, kubernetesClient, scaleGetter, scaleMapper)
   114→}
   115→
   116→func createScaleSubresourceClientGetter(kubeConfig *rest.Config) (scaleclient.ScalesGetter, meta.RESTMapper, error) {
   117→	discoveryClient, err := discovery.NewDiscoveryClientForConfig(kubeConfig)
   118→	if err != nil {
   119→		return nil, nil, err
   120→	}
   121→	scaleMapper := restmapper.NewDeferredDiscoveryRESTMapper(memory.NewMemCacheClient(discoveryClient))
   122→	scaleKindResolver := scaleclient.NewDiscoveryScaleKindResolver(discoveryClient)
   123→	client, err := scaleclient.NewForConfig(kubeConfig, scaleMapper, dynamic.LegacyAPIPathResolverFunc, scaleKindResolver)
   124→	if err != nil {
   125→		return nil, nil, err
   126→	}
   127→	return client, scaleMapper, nil
   128→}
   129→
   130→// NewCapacityBufferClientFromClients returns a CapacityBufferClient based on the passed clients
   131→func NewCapacityBufferClientFromClients(buffersClient capacitybuffer.Interface, kubernetesClient kubernetes.Interface, scaleGetter scaleclient.ScalesGetter, scaleMapper meta.RESTMapper) (*CapacityBufferClient, error) {
   132→	if buffersClient == nil || kubernetesClient == nil {
   133→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("Couldn't create capacity buffer client")
   134→	}
   135→	defaultResyncPeriod := 5 * time.Minute
   136→
   137→	stopChannel := make(chan struct{})
   138→
   139→	buffersFactory := externalversions.NewSharedInformerFactory(buffersClient, defaultResyncPeriod)
   140→	bufferInformer := buffersFactory.Autoscaling().V1beta1().CapacityBuffers().Informer()
   141→	buffersLister := buffersFactory.Autoscaling().V1beta1().CapacityBuffers().Lister()
   142→
   143→	// Add indexer for PodTemplateRef
   144→	err := bufferInformer.AddIndexers(cache.Indexers{
   145→		PodTemplateRefIndex: func(obj interface{}) ([]string, error) {
   146→			buffer, ok := obj.(*v1.CapacityBuffer)
   147→			if !ok {
   148→				return []string{}, nil
   149→			}
   150→			if buffer.Spec.PodTemplateRef != nil {
   151→				return []string{buffer.Spec.PodTemplateRef.Name}, nil
   152→			}
   153→			return []string{}, nil
   154→		},
   155→	})
   156→	if err != nil {
   157→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to add indexers: %v", err)
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	buffersFactory.Start(stopChannel)
   161→	informersSynced := buffersFactory.WaitForCacheSync(stopChannel)
   162→	for _, synced := range informersSynced {
   163→		if !synced {
   164→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("can't create buffers lister")
   165→		}
   166→	}
   167→	klog.V(2).Info("Successful initial buffers sync")
   168→
   169→	factory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(kubernetesClient, defaultResyncPeriod)
   170→	bufferClient := &CapacityBufferClient{
   171→		buffersClient:         buffersClient,
   172→		kubernetesClient:      kubernetesClient,
   173→		scaleGetter:           scaleGetter,
   174→		scaleMapper:           scaleMapper,
   175→		buffersLister:         buffersLister,
   176→		podTemplateLister:     factory.Core().V1().PodTemplates().Lister(),
   177→		replicaSetsLister:     factory.Apps().V1().ReplicaSets().Lister(),
   178→		statefulSetsLister:    factory.Apps().V1().StatefulSets().Lister(),
   179→		jobsLister:            factory.Batch().V1().Jobs().Lister(),
   180→		deploymentLister:      factory.Apps().V1().Deployments().Lister(),
   181→		replicationContLister: factory.Core().V1().ReplicationControllers().Lister(),
   182→		rqLister:              factory.Core().V1().ResourceQuotas().Lister(),
   183→		bufferInformer:        bufferInformer,
   184→		podTemplateInformer:   factory.Core().V1().PodTemplates().Informer(),
   185→		resourceQuotaInformer: factory.Core().V1().ResourceQuotas().Informer(),
   186→	}
   187→	factory.Start(stopChannel)
   188→	informersSynced = factory.WaitForCacheSync(stopChannel)
   189→	for _, synced := range informersSynced {
   190→		if !synced {
   191→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Can't initiate informer factory syncer")
   192→		}
   193→	}
   194→	return bufferClient, nil
   195→}
   196→
   197→// GetBufferInformer returns the informer for CapacityBuffer resource.
   198→func (c *CapacityBufferClient) GetBufferInformer() cache.SharedIndexInformer {
   199→	return c.bufferInformer
   200→}
